    And the target directory additionally contains a non-empty file at "features/example.feature" authored by the shop
    And the target directory additionally contains a non-empty file at "tests/test_example.py" authored by the shop
    And the target directory additionally contains a non-empty top-level file at "pyproject.toml" authored by the shop
    And the target directory additionally contains a non-empty top-level file at "README.md" authored by the shop

    Then the exit code is 0

    And the file at ".claude/agents/bc-implementer.md" in the target directory has been hand-edited so that its content differs from the current canonical "bc-implementer" template package-data file contents
    When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against the target directory "/tmp/example-shop"

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-14 21:35:56Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-14 21:36:03Z)**_

Yes — scenario `575c0f31c5e6e8e6` explicitly pins it:

> *"update overwrites hand-edits made to a bootstrap-managed agent file, restoring it to the current canonical template content byte-for-byte"*

The wrapper was treated as a hand-edit and correctly overwritten. **The behavior was right per the pinned spec.** The problem is upstream: the canonical templates in `src/shop_templates/templates/` have no frontmatter, so `update` correctly pours nothing.

**Why the frontmatter was in the wrapper in the first place:** it was hand-authored during the PDR-002 path (a) bootstrap — manually prepended to the canonical body because the `bootstrap` command didn't generate it. It was always fragile against `update`.

**Why it isn't shop-specific:** you're right. `name: bc-implementer`, `description: BC implementer role...`, and `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob` are the same for every BC shop. They belong in the canonical template, not in a local overlay.

**The fix:** add frontmatter to `src/shop_templates/templates/bc-implementer.md` and `bc-reviewer.md`. Then `update` pours it correctly everywhere.

**On the protocol question:** since this BC owns those template files, a change to them needs to flow through an inbox message per CLAUDE.md — "no editing canonical templates without an inbox message authorizing it." So this is a `request_maintenance` to the lead shop (flat change, no new capability), unless you want to authorize it directly here.

Want me to raise that with the lead shop, or are you authorizing the fix directly given you're the human directing the work?

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-messaging/.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: bc-reviewer
3	description: BC reviewer role for the shopsystem-messaging bounded context. Invoke AFTER bc-implementer has finished work on an assign_scenarios (or request_bugfix carrying scenarios) message — the BC is in its post-work state but no outbox file exists yet. The reviewer's stance is adversarial: re-runs the BDD suite, probes whether the implementation faithfully realizes scenario intent or cleverly shortcuts the literal text, probes step definitions for hidden failure modes, and is the SOLE role authorized to emit work_done (status=complete) for scenario-based work via shop-msg. Emits work_done (complete) on sign-off, clarify (scenario gap) on §4.4 escalation, or work_done (status=blocked) on implementation gap. Operates inside this BC root only.
4	tools: Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob
5	---
6	
7	You are the **BC reviewer subagent** for the shopsystem-messaging
8	bounded context. You are invoked by the main agent (router) after the
9	bc-implementer subagent has finished its turn on an `assign_scenarios`
10	or scenario-carrying `request_bugfix` message. You are the gate.
```
</details></tool-use>

        By default, all communication is in bytes, and therefore any "input" should

        according to locale encoding, or by "encoding" if set. Text mode is

- Update reads shop type from `.claude/shop/type.md` (no `--shop-type` flag), overwrites CLAUDE.md and `.claude/canonical/<shop_type>-primer.md`, leaves the `.claude/shop/` user-owned files alone
- Scenario c458502d8632952b explicitly contradicts and retires the existing scenario `56a0ac7107ba5c15` (update's CLAUDE.md non-touch invariant)

    \ content\n  Given an existing git repository at a target directory \"<target>\"\
    \ with no \".claude/shop/\" subdirectory\n  When I invoke the \"shop-templates\"\
    \ bootstrap entry point with shop type \"<shop_type>\", shop name \"<shop_name>\"\
    , and target directory \"<target>\"\n  Then the exit code is 0\n  And the target\
    \ directory contains a file at \".claude/shop/type.md\"\n  And the byte contents\
    \ of that file are exactly the literal string \"<shop_type>\" with a single trailing\
    \ newline and no other content\n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name   \
    \            | target                       |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging\
    \    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |\n    | lead      | shopsystem-product  \
    \    | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |\n\n"

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/shop_templates/cli.py`

```text
1	"""shop-templates CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	    list
5	        Print available role-template names (one per line) to stdout.
6	    show <name>
7	        Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
8	        with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
9	    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
10	        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
11	        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
12	        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
13	        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
14	        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
15	    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
16	        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
17	        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
18	        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
19	        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
20	        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.
21	
22	Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
23	files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
24	The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
25	is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
26	consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import argparse
31	import subprocess
32	import sys
33	from importlib.resources import files
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	
37	_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
38	_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"
39	_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude_settings"
40	
41	# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
42	# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
43	# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
44	# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
45	# canonical set.
46	_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
47	    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
48	    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
49	}
50	
51	_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())
52	
53	
54	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
55	# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
56	#
57	# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
58	# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
59	# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
60	# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
61	# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
62	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	
65	def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
66	    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.
67	
68	    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
69	    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
70	    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
71	    gitignore template) are added.
72	    """
73	    pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
74	    names = []
75	    for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
76	        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
77	        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
78	        if not resource.is_file():
79	            continue
80	        name = resource.name
81	        if name.endswith(".md"):
82	            names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
83	    return sorted(names)
84	
85	
86	def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
87	    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
88	    try:
89	        return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
90	    except FileNotFoundError:
91	        return None
92	
93	
94	def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
95	    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.
96	
97	    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
98	    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
99	    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
100	    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
101	    CLAUDE.md.
102	    """
103	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
104	        raise ValueError(
105	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
106	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
107	        )
108	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
109	    return resource.read_text()
110	
111	
112	def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
113	    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
114	    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()
115	
116	
117	def read_claude_settings_template(shop_type: str) -> str:
118	    """Return the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template body for a shop type.
119	
120	    Per scenarios 1621b59b0ea8b20b and family (brief 003 §A): the body is
121	    loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is never read
122	    from a filesystem path under the product working directory. The
123	    returned string is the source of truth from which the bootstrap
124	    entry point generates a target directory's ".claude/settings.json"
125	    for a shop of the given type, and is also what the update entry
126	    point re-pours.
127	
128	    Per scenarios 679d227f04533ad4, c1e7f31eeef73e05, and 287e6a4f31533336:
129	    the body's content discipline (SessionStart hook shape, watch
130	    target per shop type, the `bd prime` companion hook on lead,
131	    alias-bypassing grep, exclusion of the other shop type's watch
132	    target) is a property of the package-data file itself — this
133	    accessor is a pure pass-through.
134	    """
135	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
136	        raise ValueError(
137	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
138	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
139	        )
140	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.json"
141	    return resource.read_text()
142	
143	
144	def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
145	    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.
146	
147	    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
148	    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
149	    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
150	    """
151	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
152	        raise ValueError(
153	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
154	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
155	        )
156	    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]
157	
158	
159	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
160	# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
161	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
162	
163	
164	def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
165	    for name in _list_template_names():
166	        print(name)
167	    return 0
168	
169	
170	def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
171	    content = _read_template(args.name)
172	    if content is None:
173	        available = ", ".join(_list_template_names())
174	        print(
175	            f"shop-templates show: no template named {args.name!r}. Available: {available}",
176	            file=sys.stderr,
177	        )
178	        return 1
179	    # Use sys.stdout.write to preserve exact bytes (no trailing newline from print).
180	    sys.stdout.write(content)
181	    return 0
182	
183	
184	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
185	# `bootstrap` subcommand
186	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
187	
188	
189	def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
190	    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
191	    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
192	    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
193	    """
194	    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
195	    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)
196	
197	
198	def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
199	    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.
200	
201	    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
202	    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
203	    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
204	    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
205	    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
206	
207	    Per scenario 32d99f6d4a2dad37 (lead-2gr round 2 of lead-k8v's
208	    bd-init side-effect closure), `bd init` MUST be invoked with the
209	    `--skip-agents` flag. That flag is bd's own opt-out for the
210	    AGENTS.md / .claude/settings.json / CLAUDE.md-beads-block append
211	    that bd otherwise applies during `bd init`. The shop's canonical
212	    agent surface is .claude/agents/*.md alone (poured by bootstrap
213	    itself); bd's agent-surface generation is duplicative at best and
214	    contradictory at worst, so we suppress it. Verified against bd
215	    1.0.3: `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init --skip-agents --quiet` in a
216	    fresh probe dir produces .beads/ + .gitignore and no AGENTS.md,
217	    no .claude/.
218	    """
219	    result = subprocess.run(
220	        ["bd", "init", "--skip-agents"],
221	        cwd=str(target),
222	        capture_output=True,
223	        text=True,
224	    )
225	    if result.returncode != 0:
226	        print(
227	            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
228	            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
229	            file=sys.stderr,
230	        )
231	    return result.returncode
232	
233	
234	def _validate_shop_type(shop_type: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
235	    """Return None if shop_type is valid; else print a usage diagnostic to
236	    stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
237	
238	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios d6e935bca62d0039 (missing
239	    shop type) and 7941cdc591cd2c3b (invalid shop type): stderr names
240	    the offending input AND lists the accepted values "bc" and "lead".
241	    """
242	    accepted = ", ".join(f'"{s}"' for s in _SHOP_TYPES)
243	    if shop_type is None:
244	        print(
245	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: shop type. "
246	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
247	            file=sys.stderr,
248	        )
249	        return "missing"
250	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
251	        print(
252	            f"shop-templates {command}: invalid shop type {shop_type!r}. "
253	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
254	            file=sys.stderr,
255	        )
256	        return "invalid"
257	    return None
258	
259	
260	def _validate_shop_name(shop_name: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
261	    """Return None if shop_name is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
262	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
263	
264	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenario 3c8612d20608e9a3: stderr
265	    names "--shop-name" as the missing required argument and emits no
266	    Python traceback / no "TypeError" substring. We validate manually
267	    (matching the in-house pattern used by `_validate_shop_type`) rather
268	    than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is ours, not
269	    argparse's auto-generated message.
270	    """
271	    if shop_name is None:
272	        print(
273	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --shop-name. "
274	            f"Provide the shop's own name (e.g. shopsystem-messaging).",
275	            file=sys.stderr,
276	        )
277	        return "missing"
278	    return None
279	
280	
281	def _validate_target(target: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
282	    """Return None if target is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
283	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
284	
285	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios fe59a11a88a9ab60
286	    (bootstrap) and 8fe363bd46cb766c (update): stderr names "--target"
287	    as the missing required argument and emits no Python traceback / no
288	    "TypeError" substring. We validate manually (matching the in-house
289	    pattern used by `_validate_shop_type` and `_validate_shop_name`)
290	    rather than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is
291	    ours, not argparse's auto-generated message — and so the
292	    diagnostic surfaces BEFORE any Path(args.target) construction that
293	    would otherwise raise TypeError when target is None.
294	    """
295	    if target is None:
296	        print(
297	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --target. "
298	            f"Provide the path to the target repository.",
299	            file=sys.stderr,
300	        )
301	        return "missing"
302	    return None
303	
304	
305	def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
306	    # Validate shop_type early so we can emit the usage diagnostic shape
307	    # the scenarios pin (named offending input + list of accepted values)
308	    # BEFORE touching the target directory. Scenarios d6e935bca62d0039
309	    # and 7941cdc591cd2c3b both require "writes no scaffold" when the
310	    # shop type is bad.
311	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "bootstrap")
312	    if err is not None:
313	        return 2
314	
315	    # Validate shop_name early for the same reason. Scenario
316	    # 3c8612d20608e9a3 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--shop-name" + no
317	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --shop-name is omitted.
318	    # Without this guard, args.shop_name=None propagates into
319	    # _render_claude_md and surfaces as a TypeError from str.replace().
320	    err = _validate_shop_name(args.shop_name, "bootstrap")
321	    if err is not None:
322	        return 2
323	
324	    # Validate target early for the same reason. Scenario
325	    # fe59a11a88a9ab60 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--target" + no
326	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --target is omitted. Without
327	    # this guard, args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces
328	    # as a TypeError from pathlib.
329	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "bootstrap")
330	    if err is not None:
331	        return 2
332	
333	    target = Path(args.target)
334	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
335	    shop_name: str = args.shop_name
336	
337	    # Pour role-prompt files into .claude/agents/.
338	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
339	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
340	    for role_name in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]:
341	        body = _read_template(role_name)
342	        if body is None:
343	            print(
344	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: internal error — canonical "
345	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
346	                file=sys.stderr,
347	            )
348	            return 1
349	        (agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md").write_text(body)
350	
351	    # Top-level CLAUDE.md — render the primer with the shop's identity.
352	    (target / "CLAUDE.md").write_text(_render_claude_md(shop_type, shop_name))
353	
354	    # Top-level .gitignore — pour the canonical template.
355	    (target / ".gitignore").write_text(read_gitignore_template())
356	
357	    # .claude/settings.json — pour the canonical per-shop-type settings
358	    # template (SessionStart activation hook over the shop's inbound
359	    # mailbox surface). Per brief 003 scope item A (scenarios
360	    # 1621b59b0ea8b20b, c8002527857e0dd1, f83e03ee69261242): the
361	    # canonical settings template lives in package data and bootstrap
362	    # writes it into the target's .claude/settings.json byte-for-byte.
363	    claude_dir = target / ".claude"
364	    claude_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
365	    (claude_dir / "settings.json").write_text(
366	        read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)
367	    )
368	
369	    # Initialize .beads/ via a `bd init` subprocess. shop-templates MUST
370	    # NOT import bd / beads internals and MUST NOT write to .beads/
371	    # directly (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e).
372	    rc = _bd_init_in(target)
373	    if rc != 0:
374	        return rc
375	
376	    # Lead-shop bootstrap installs each sibling BC clone under repos/
377	    # editable into the product venv, per brief 003 scope item E
378	    # (scenarios 3957f255c35aff60 outcome pin / ff882696856530a4
379	    # mechanism pin). The reference realization: for each subdir of
380	    # target/repos/ that ships a Python package (pyproject.toml or
381	    # setup.py), pip-install it editable into target/.venv/.
382	    if shop_type == "lead":
383	        rc = _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable(target)
384	        if rc != 0:
385	            return rc
386	
387	    return 0
388	
389	
390	def _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable(target: Path) -> int:
391	    """For a lead shop, install every sibling BC clone under target/repos/
392	    editable into target/.venv/.
393	
394	    Per brief 003 scope item E (scenarios 3957f255c35aff60 /
395	    ff882696856530a4): on session start in a bootstrapped lead shop,
396	    every BC CLI installed into the product venv must resolve invocations
397	    to the current source tree of that BC's clone under repos/, so a
398	    source edit in a BC clone is reflected in the next CLI invocation
399	    without an intervening manual reinstall. The reference realization
400	    is `pip install -e` per BC-clone subdir.
401	
402	    Well-defined no-op states (returns 0 silently):
403	      - target/repos/ does not exist (lead-shop bootstrap with no BC
404	        clones yet — venv creation is also a no-op here).
405	      - target/repos/ exists but contains no installable subdir.
406	
407	    If at least one installable subdir is present, ensure the venv
408	    exists (creating it on demand) and install each installable subdir
409	    in editable mode.
410	    """
411	    repos_dir = target / "repos"
412	    if not repos_dir.exists() or not repos_dir.is_dir():
413	        return 0
414	
415	    # Filter to installable subdirs first; defer venv creation until
416	    # we know we have at least one clone to install.
417	    installable_children = []
418	    for child in sorted(repos_dir.iterdir()):
419	        if not child.is_dir():
420	            continue
421	        if (
422	            (child / "pyproject.toml").exists()
423	            or (child / "setup.py").exists()
424	            or (child / "setup.cfg").exists()
425	        ):
426	            installable_children.append(child)
427	
428	    if not installable_children:
429	        return 0
430	
431	    venv_dir = target / ".venv"
432	    if not venv_dir.exists():
433	        result = subprocess.run(
434	            [sys.executable, "-m", "venv", str(venv_dir)],
435	            capture_output=True,
436	            text=True,
437	        )
438	        if result.returncode != 0:
439	            print(
440	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: failed to create venv at "
441	                f"{venv_dir!s} (exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n"
442	                f"{result.stderr}",
443	                file=sys.stderr,
444	            )
445	            return result.returncode
446	
447	    venv_python = venv_dir / "bin" / "python"
448	    if not venv_python.exists():
449	        # Windows or other layouts — fall back to Scripts/python.exe
450	        alt = venv_dir / "Scripts" / "python.exe"
451	        if alt.exists():
452	            venv_python = alt
453	        else:
454	            print(
455	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: cannot find venv python "
456	                f"interpreter under {venv_dir!s}",
457	                file=sys.stderr,
458	            )
459	            return 1
460	
461	    for child in installable_children:
462	        result = subprocess.run(
463	            [str(venv_python), "-m", "pip", "install", "-e", str(child)],
464	            capture_output=True,
465	            text=True,
466	        )
467	        if result.returncode != 0:
468	            print(
469	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: `pip install -e {child!s}` "
470	                f"failed (exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n"
471	                f"{result.stderr}",
472	                file=sys.stderr,
473	            )
474	            return result.returncode
475	
476	    return 0
477	
478	
479	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
480	# `update` subcommand
481	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
482	
483	
484	def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
485	    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
486	    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
487	    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
488	    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
489	    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
490	    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
491	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
492	    if err is not None:
493	        return 2
494	
495	    # Validate target early. Scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c pins exit 2 +
496	    # stderr names "--target" + no traceback + every file under the
497	    # previously-bootstrapped shop's .claude/agents/ is byte+mtime
498	    # identical when --target is omitted. Without this guard,
499	    # args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces as a
500	    # TypeError from pathlib.
501	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")
502	    if err is not None:
503	        return 2
504	
505	    target = Path(args.target)
506	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
507	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
508	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
509	
510	    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])
511	
512	    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
513	    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
514	    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
515	    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
516	    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
517	    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
518	    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
519	    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
520	    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
521	    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
522	    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
523	    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
524	    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
525	    all_canonical_names = set()
526	    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
527	        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
528	    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
529	    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
530	    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
531	    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
532	    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
533	    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
534	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
535	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
536	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
537	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
538	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
539	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
540	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
541	        if "-" not in stem:
542	            return False
543	        if stem != stem.lower():
544	            return False
545	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
546	            return False
547	        return True
548	
549	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
550	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
551	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
552	        if not child.is_file():
553	            continue
554	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
555	            continue
556	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
557	        if stem in canonical:
558	            continue
559	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
560	            child.unlink()
561	
562	    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
563	    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
564	    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
565	    for role_name in canonical:
566	        body = _read_template(role_name)
567	        if body is None:
568	            print(
569	                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
570	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
571	                file=sys.stderr,
572	            )
573	            return 1
574	        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
575	        if target_file.exists():
576	            current = target_file.read_text()
577	            if current == body:
578	                continue
579	        target_file.write_text(body)
580	
581	    # Step 3: re-pour the canonical .claude/settings.json. Per scenarios
582	    # d29cd723439faae1 and d3066d4476d0a975: settings.json is treated as
583	    # bootstrap-managed (not init-only) — stale content is replaced and
584	    # already-current content is left byte-for-byte unchanged. This is
585	    # additive to the prior update contract: the non-touch invariant on
586	    # CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/ (scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15,
587	    # ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7, efae77e534588357) still holds.
588	    canonical_settings = read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)
589	    settings_file = target / ".claude" / "settings.json"
590	    settings_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
591	    if settings_file.exists() and settings_file.read_text() == canonical_settings:
592	        pass  # leave byte+mtime unchanged
593	    else:
594	        settings_file.write_text(canonical_settings)
595	
596	    return 0
597	
598	
599	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
600	# Argparse wiring
601	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
602	
603	
604	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
605	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
606	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
607	
608	    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
609	    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)
610	
611	    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
612	    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
613	    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)
614	
615	    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
616	        "bootstrap",
617	        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
618	    )
619	    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
620	    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
621	    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
622	    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
623	    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
624	    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
625	    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
626	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
627	        "--shop-type",
628	        choices=None,  # validate manually so error shape is ours, not argparse's
629	        default=None,
630	        help='one of "bc" or "lead"',
631	    )
632	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
633	        "--shop-name",
634	        default=None,
635	        help="the shop's own name, e.g. shopsystem-messaging",
636	    )
637	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
638	        "--target",
639	        default=None,
640	        help="path to the existing repository to scaffold",
641	    )
642	    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)
643	
644	    update_cmd = sub.add_parser(
645	        "update",
646	        help="re-pour bootstrap-managed agent files from current package data",
647	    )
648	    update_cmd.add_argument(
649	        "--target",
650	        default=None,
651	        help="path to the previously-bootstrapped repository",
652	    )
653	    update_cmd.add_argument(
654	        "--shop-type",
655	        default=None,
656	        help='one of "bc" or "lead" (must match the original bootstrap)',
657	    )
658	    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)
659	
660	    return parser
661	
662	
663	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
664	    parser = build_parser()
665	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
666	    return args.func(args)
667	
668	
669	if __name__ == "__main__":
670	    sys.exit(main())
671
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	"""Shared fixtures and pytest-bdd step definitions for shopsystem-templates.
2	
3	The scenarios under features/ pin the BC's outward contract — the
4	`shop-templates` CLI surface and the structural section headers each
5	role-template markdown file carries. The CLI is invoked via subprocess
6	(the same boundary downstream callers use); content checks read the
7	template via the same CLI subcommand so any drift between the CLI's
8	view of the package data and the file system surfaces here.
9	
10	Style mirrors shopsystem-messaging/tests/conftest.py and
11	shopsystem-scenarios/tests/conftest.py — subprocess + a `context` dict
12	fixture carrying cross-step state.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import re
17	import subprocess
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	import pytest
22	from pytest_bdd import given, parsers, then, when
23	
24	
25	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# Shared cross-step state
27	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	
30	@pytest.fixture
31	def context() -> dict:
32	    return {}
33	
34	
35	def _run_shop_templates(*args: str) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
36	    return subprocess.run(
37	        ["shop-templates", *args],
38	        capture_output=True,
39	        text=True,
40	    )
41	
42	
43	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Given steps
45	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
46	
47	
48	@given(
49	    parsers.parse(
50	        'a template name "{name}" that is in the list output by '
51	        '"shop-templates list"'
52	    )
53	)
54	def given_template_name_in_list(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
55	    # Verify empirically that the name appears in `shop-templates list`
56	    # output — the Given's contract is part of the scenario's premise and
57	    # a future drop of a template would silently undermine the When/Then
58	    # steps if we didn't check it here.
59	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
60	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
61	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
62	    )
63	    listed = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
64	    assert name in listed, (
65	        f"premise of Given violated: {name!r} is not in shop-templates list "
66	        f"output {listed!r}"
67	    )
68	    context["available_templates"] = listed
69	
70	
71	@given(
72	    parsers.parse(
73	        'a template name "{name}" that is not in the list output by '
74	        '"shop-templates list"'
75	    )
76	)
77	def given_template_name_not_in_list(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
78	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
79	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
80	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
81	    )
82	    listed = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
83	    assert name not in listed, (
84	        f"premise of Given violated: {name!r} unexpectedly appears in "
85	        f"shop-templates list output {listed!r}"
86	    )
87	    context["available_templates"] = listed
88	
89	
90	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
91	# When steps
92	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
93	
94	
95	@when(parsers.parse('I run "shop-templates list"'))
96	def when_run_list(context: dict) -> None:
97	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
98	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
99	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
100	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
```

112	@when(

115	    )
116	)
117	def when_read_template_via_show(name: str, name_again: str, context: dict) -> None:
118	    # Belt-and-braces: the two name slots must agree, otherwise the
119	    # scenario text is internally inconsistent and any Then assertion
120	    # would be assertion-by-coincidence.
121	    assert name == name_again, (
122	        f"scenario inconsistency: 'the {name} template' but "
123	        f"'shop-templates show {name_again}'"
124	    )
125	    result = _run_shop_templates("show", name)
126	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
127	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
128	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
129	    context["shown_template_name"] = name
130	    # The Then-steps for these scenarios refer to "the content" — keep an
131	    # explicit alias so the assertion language matches the feature text.

138	
139	
140	@then("the exit code is 0")

142	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]

150	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]
151	    assert rc != 0, (

153	    )
154	
155	
156	@then("stderr is empty")
157	def then_stderr_is_empty(context: dict) -> None:
158	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
159	    assert stderr == "", f"expected empty stderr; got {stderr!r}"
160	
161	
162	@then("stdout is empty")
163	def then_stdout_is_empty(context: dict) -> None:
164	    stdout = context["cli_stdout"]
165	    assert stdout == "", f"expected empty stdout; got {stdout!r}"
166	
167	
168	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
169	# Then steps — shop-templates list shape
170	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
171	
172	
173	@then(
174	    parsers.re(
175	        r'stdout is exactly the four names '
176	        r'"(?P<n1>[^"]+)", "(?P<n2>[^"]+)", "(?P<n3>[^"]+)", "(?P<n4>[^"]+)", '
177	        r'one per line in that sorted order'
178	    )
179	)
180	def then_stdout_is_exactly_four_names_sorted(
181	    n1: str, n2: str, n3: str, n4: str, context: dict
182	) -> None:
183	    expected = [n1, n2, n3, n4]
184	    # Pin the sorted-order contract explicitly: if the scenario text
185	    # claims "sorted order" but the literal sequence supplied is not
186	    # actually sorted, the scenario is internally inconsistent and the
187	    # downstream assertion would be meaningless.
188	    assert expected == sorted(expected), (
189	        f"scenario premise violated: supplied names {expected!r} are not "
190	        f"in sorted order"
191	    )
192	    stdout = context["cli_stdout"]
193	    # The CLI uses print() per line, which emits a trailing newline after
194	    # the last line. Split on newlines and drop the final empty entry
195	    # that comes from that trailing newline.
196	    lines = stdout.split("\n")
197	    if lines and lines[-1] == "":
198	        lines = lines[:-1]
199	    assert lines == expected, (
200	        f"expected stdout lines {expected!r}; got {lines!r}"
201	    )
202	
203	
204	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
205	# Then steps — shop-templates show byte-for-byte equivalence
206	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
207	
208	
209	@then(
210	    "stdout equals the package-data file contents for that template, "
211	    "byte-for-byte (no extra trailing newline beyond what the file itself carries)"
212	)
213	def then_stdout_equals_package_data_byte_for_byte(context: dict) -> None:
214	    # Re-read the underlying template through the same package-data
215	    # boundary the CLI uses. Importing inside the step (rather than at
216	    # module top) keeps the conftest cleanly isolated from src/ — only
217	    # this assertion needs the boundary helper, and only at run time.
218	    from shop_templates.cli import _read_template
219	
220	    name = context["shown_template_name"]
221	    expected = _read_template(name)
222	    assert expected is not None, (
223	        f"premise of Then violated: package data has no template named {name!r}"
224	    )
225	    actual = context["cli_stdout"]
226	    assert actual == expected, (
227	        f"shop-templates show {name} did not emit byte-identical content "
228	        f"to the package-data file.\n"
229	        f"len(expected)={len(expected)} len(actual)={len(actual)}\n"
230	        f"expected_repr_tail={expected[-40:]!r}\n"
231	        f"actual_repr_tail={actual[-40:]!r}"
232	    )
233	
234	
235	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
236	# Then steps — shop-templates show unknown-template error shape
237	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
238	
239	
240	@then(parsers.parse('stderr names the offending input "{name}"'))
241	def then_stderr_names_offending_input(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
242	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
243	    assert name in stderr, (
244	        f"expected stderr to name offending input {name!r}; got:\n{stderr}"
245	    )
246	
247	
248	@then(
249	    "stderr cites the list of available templates so the caller can recover"
250	)
251	def then_stderr_cites_available_templates(context: dict) -> None:
252	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
253	    # "cites the list" is satisfied when at least one real template name
254	    # appears in stderr — the caller can derive the available set from

258	    available = context.get("available_templates")

The two cannot both pass — making the new scenario pass requires deleting or retiring 56a0ac7107ba5c15.

74	    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "/tmp/example-shop" with no ".beads/" directory
75	    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "bc", shop name "example-shop", and target directory "/tmp/example-shop"

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-19 13:26:50Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Read the existing bc CLAUDE.md primer template

`cat /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md`

```text
# {{SHOP_NAME}} — BC shop instructions

This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** Bounded Context shop. As an agent
operating in this repo, you are operating inside a **BC shop** that uses
the inbox/outbox message protocol from §4 of the shop-system spec.

## Who you are — router for bc-implementer and bc-reviewer subagents

By default you are the **router** for this BC shop. The two role-discipline
positions — **Implementer** and **Reviewer** per the shop-system spec §4 /
§4.4 — are dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request
and delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.

The canonical role set for this shop type is:

- **bc-implementer** — reads inbox messages, applies the sufficiency check
  matching the message type, and either emits `clarify` via `shop-msg
  respond clarify` or does the work (feature file under `features/`, step
  defs in `tests/conftest.py`, implementation under `src/`, BDD passing).

- **bc-reviewer** — dispatched AFTER the implementer's turn on an
  `assign_scenarios` (or scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`) message has
  finished and the BC is in its post-work state with no outbox file yet.
  The reviewer is the sole role authorized to emit `work_done` for
  scenario-based work.

Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md` and
`.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`. These are inline copies of the canonical
templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not edit them
independently of the canonical source.

## BC inbox / outbox protocol

- **Inbox** (`inbox/`) holds messages from the lead shop. Filename
  convention: `<work_id>.yaml`. One file per dispatch.
- **Outbox** (`outbox/`) holds this BC's responses. Filename convention:
  `<work_id>-<response_type>.yaml`. The `shop-msg respond` CLI builds
  and validates these — never write outbox YAML by hand.
- A message is considered **unprocessed** when there is no outbox file
  for its `work_id`. Use `shop-msg pending inbox` to identify
  unprocessed work, and `shop-msg read inbox` to read a specific
  inbox message.

## Do not

- **No editing the BC's inbox/outbox by hand.** `shop-msg send` writes
  inboxes (lead shop's job); `shop-msg respond` writes outboxes
  (BC's job). Both validate against the schema.
- **No skipping the sufficiency check.** Each BC role template carries
  a sufficiency check matching the inbound message type; honor it.

## Beads (bd) discipline

This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. The
inbound `work_id` on each inbox message is the lead shop's bead ID;
this BC's own follow-up findings (mechanism observations, escaped
risks, deferred work) are filed as beads in this repo.

- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
  an issue, and `bd close <id>` to mark work complete. Do NOT track
  work in markdown TODO lists or alternative trackers.
- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
  session.

## Session start: arming the inbox watcher via the in-session Monitor

This shop is reactive on session start: when a new message lands in
`inbox/`, the router must learn about it without polling. The activation
mechanism is the in-session **Monitor** tool — not a `SessionStart` hook
in `.claude/settings.json`. (Earlier iterations of this template tried
the hook path; Claude Code awaits `SessionStart` hooks synchronously, so
a foreground `inotifywait -m` pipeline never returns and session startup
hangs. The Monitor tool is the documented in-session primitive that
delivers the streaming-stdout-as-notifications semantic the hook was
faking, without blocking startup.)

**At session start, the router must arm the in-session Monitor tool on
the following pipeline (watch target: `inbox/`):**

```
stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to inbox/
```

Before arming the Monitor, the router must verify that both
`inotifywait` and `stdbuf` are on PATH (for example, via
`command -v inotifywait` and `command -v stdbuf`). If either executable
is missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a
visible diagnostic naming the missing prerequisite — do not silently
fall back to a no-watcher state, and do not arm the watcher via a
`SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` as a fallback. A
no-watcher session loses the shop's reactivity invariant; the operator
needs to see the diagnostic and install the missing package.

### Host prerequisites

The Monitor activation pipeline named above depends on two host-level
packages being present on PATH:

- **inotify-tools** — provides the `inotifywait` binary used to watch
  the inbound mailbox surface for new messages.
- **coreutils** — provides `stdbuf`, used to set line-buffered output
  on the `inotifywait` invocation so events stream as they happen
  rather than batching into a pipe buffer.

Install both packages through your distro's package manager (e.g.
`apt-get install inotify-tools coreutils` on Debian/Ubuntu).
```
</details></tool-use>

# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions

This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** lead shop. As an agent operating
in this repo, you are operating inside a **lead shop** under the shop-system
spec.

## Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents

By default you are the **router** for this lead shop. The two judgment
roles — **PO** and **Architect** per §3 of the shop-system spec — are
dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request and
delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.

The canonical role set for this shop type is:

- **lead-po** — authors or sharpens Gherkin scenarios; drafts briefs or
  PDRs; responds to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.

- **lead-architect** — selects a message-type vehicle; composes
  `shop-msg send`; verifies BC pre-state empirically; responds to BC
  `clarify` on architecture; reconciles scenario registers; drafts ADRs;
  makes BC decomposition decisions.

Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` and
`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`. These are inline copies of the
canonical templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not
edit them independently of the canonical source.

## How feature requests get handled
```

2401	            )

2406	
2407	
2408	# =======================================================================
2409	# Step definitions — bootstrap_cli_surface.feature (lead-k8v dispatch)
2410	# =======================================================================
2411	#
2412	# Scenarios pin the `shop-templates bootstrap` and `shop-templates update`
2413	# CLI surface that scaffolds and reconciles an existing shop repository:
2414	# .claude/agents/<role>.md (role-prompt copies), top-level CLAUDE.md
2415	# (shop primer), top-level .gitignore, and .beads/ (initialized via a
2416	# `bd init` subprocess).
2417	#
2418	# Two cross-cutting test concerns are handled by helpers below:
2419	#
2420	#   1. Target directories. Scenarios spell paths as "/tmp/example-shop"
2421	#      etc., which are shared across scenarios and not safe to use
2422	#      literally (they would leak state between tests, and tests would
2423	#      stomp on real /tmp/). The Given step redirects every named
2424	#      target to a fresh pytest tmp directory and stores the mapping in
2425	#      context["target_alias_to_real"]. Subsequent steps that name the
2426	#      same alias resolve to the same real path.
2427	#
2428	#   2. Subprocess observation. Scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e requires that
2429	#      `bd init` is executed as a subprocess (not by importing bd in-
2430	#      process), and scenario 3f4d7d2256a97ae7 requires that update
2431	#      executes NO `bd` subprocess. We install a per-test shim
2432	#      directory at the front of PATH containing a fake `bd` script
2433	#      that (a) logs every invocation to a file and (b) creates the
2434	#      .beads/ directory in cwd (so `bd init` still produces the
2435	#      side-effect bootstrap callers depend on). After the invocation
2436	#      we read the log to check what subprocesses ran and which import
2437	#      check the shim recorded.
2438	
2439	
2440	_BOOTSTRAP_TARGET_ALIASES = (
2441	    "/tmp/example-shop",
2442	    "/tmp/example-bc-shop",
2443	    "/tmp/example-lead-shop",
2444	)
2445	
2446	
2447	def _real_target_for_alias(alias: str, context: dict) -> Path:
2448	    """Map a feature-file path alias (e.g. "/tmp/example-shop") to a
2449	    fresh per-test directory. Subsequent calls with the same alias
2450	    within a single scenario resolve to the same real path.
2451	    """
2452	    mapping = context.setdefault("target_alias_to_real", {})
2453	    if alias in mapping:
2454	        return mapping[alias]
2455	    # We allocate per-alias subdirectories inside a single pytest tmp
2456	    # path stash on context, so each scenario gets an isolated workspace.
2457	    base = context.get("bootstrap_workspace")
2458	    assert base is not None, (
2459	        "Given step ordering bug: target alias resolved before "
2460	        "bootstrap_workspace was prepared"
2461	    )
2462	    real = base / alias.lstrip("/").replace("/", "_")
2463	    real.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2464	    # Initialize as a git repo so the Given's "existing git repository"
2465	    # premise holds.
2466	    subprocess.run(
2467	        ["git", "init", "-q", str(real)],
2468	        check=True,
2469	        capture_output=True,
2470	    )
2471	    mapping[alias] = real
2472	    return real
2473	
2474	
2475	@pytest.fixture(autouse=False)
2476	def _ensure_bootstrap_workspace(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2477	    """Not autouse; invoked from Given steps that need a fresh workspace."""
2478	    context["bootstrap_workspace"] = tmp_path
2479	    return tmp_path
2480	
2481	
2482	def _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path: Path, context: dict) -> Path:
2483	    """Create a directory containing a fake `bd` executable that logs
2484	    invocations to a file and minimally simulates `bd init` (mkdir
2485	    .beads/ in cwd with a marker file inside). Return the directory
2486	    path so the caller can prepend it to PATH.
2487	
2488	    Per scenarios 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e and 3f4d7d2256a97ae7, tests need to
2489	    answer "did a `bd` subprocess run, and with what first argument".
2490	    The log file path is stashed on context as "bd_shim_log" and is
2491	    TRUNCATED on each call — so only the most-recent invocation's bd
2492	    calls are observable. (Tests that need to chain a Given-side
2493	    bootstrap into a When-side update don't want the Given-side `bd
2494	    init` call to pollute the When-side observation window.)
2495	    """
2496	    shim_dir = tmp_path / "bd-shim"
2497	    shim_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
2498	    log = tmp_path / "bd-shim.log"
2499	    # Truncate so each invocation observes only its own subprocess calls.
2500	    log.write_text("")
2501	    context["bd_shim_log"] = log
2502	    bd_path = shim_dir / "bd"
2503	    # The shim is intentionally tiny — it logs argv (one line per call:
2504	    # "<arg1> <arg2> ..." plus its cwd) and, if the first arg is "init",
2505	    # creates ".beads/__shim_init__" inside cwd as the visible
2506	    # side-effect bootstrap callers depend on.
2507	    bd_path.write_text(
2508	        "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
2509	        f"log={log}\n"
2510	        'echo "$@|cwd=$PWD" >> "$log"\n'
2511	        'if [[ "$1" == "init" ]]; then\n'
2512	        '  mkdir -p .beads\n'
2513	        '  : > .beads/__shim_init__\n'
2514	        'fi\n'
2515	        "exit 0\n"
2516	    )
2517	    bd_path.chmod(0o755)
2518	    return shim_dir
2519	
2520	
2521	def _run_shop_templates_with_bd_shim(
2522	    args: list[str], context: dict, tmp_path: Path
2523	) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
2524	    """Invoke `shop-templates` with a fake `bd` on PATH, capturing
2525	    whether/how `bd` was called and whether `bd`/`beads` Python modules
2526	    got imported into the shop-templates process.
2527	
2528	    The "no bd/beads imports" check (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e) is
2529	    implemented by invoking shop-templates indirectly via a small
2530	    Python wrapper that (1) runs `shop_templates.cli.main(args)` and
2531	    (2) after main returns, writes `bd_in_modules=True/False` and
2532	    `beads_in_modules=True/False` to an import-trace log. The test
2533	    asserts both are False.
2534	    """
2535	    import os
2536	    import shutil
2537	
2538	    shim_dir = _make_bd_shim_dir(tmp_path, context)
2539	    import_log = tmp_path / "import-trace.log"
2540	    context["import_trace_log"] = import_log
2541	
2542	    # Build a wrapper script that runs shop_templates.cli.main(argv)
2543	    # and then writes the import-trace flags. We must NOT import bd
2544	    # or beads ourselves anywhere in this wrapper (the assertion would
2545	    # then trivially fail).
2546	    wrapper = tmp_path / "_run_shop_templates_wrapper.py"
2547	    args_repr = repr(list(args))
2548	    wrapper.write_text(
2549	        "import sys\n"
2550	        "from shop_templates.cli import main\n"
2551	        f"argv = {args_repr}\n"
2552	        "rc = main(argv)\n"
2553	        "import_log = open(r%r, 'w')\n" % str(import_log)
2554	        + "bd_in = any(\n"
2555	        "    m == 'bd' or m.startswith('bd.')\n"
2556	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2557	        ")\n"
2558	        "beads_in = any(\n"
2559	        "    m == 'beads' or m.startswith('beads.')\n"
2560	        "    for m in sys.modules\n"
2561	        ")\n"
2562	        "import_log.write(f'bd_in_modules={bd_in}\\n')\n"
2563	        "import_log.write(f'beads_in_modules={beads_in}\\n')\n"
2564	        "import_log.close()\n"
2565	        "sys.exit(rc)\n"
2566	    )
2567	
2568	    env = os.environ.copy()
2569	    env["PATH"] = f"{shim_dir}{os.pathsep}{env.get('PATH', '')}"
2570	
2571	    # Use the same python interpreter that's running the test, so
2572	    # `shop_templates` is importable from the installed package.
2573	    py = sys.executable
2574	    return subprocess.run(
2575	        [py, str(wrapper)],
2576	        capture_output=True,
2577	        text=True,
2578	        env=env,
2579	    )
2580	
2581

2583	# Given steps — target directory premise
2584	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

2590	    )
2591	)
2592	def given_existing_git_repo_at_target(
2593	    alias: str, context: dict, tmp_path: Path
2594	) -> None:
2595	    context["bootstrap_workspace"] = tmp_path
2596	    _real_target_for_alias(alias, context)
2597

2599	@given(

2974	# Bindings for the scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031 placeholders. The

2977	# Examples) — they are placeholder names meant for the test harness

2982	_PLACEHOLDER_FORMER_BC_ROLE = "bc-legacy-deprecated"
2983	_PLACEHOLDER_NEW_BC_ROLE = "bc-implementer"

2986	def _bind_role_placeholder(literal: str) -> str:
2987	    """Substitute scenario-level role-name placeholders with the

2990	    only the bracketed token and keep the rest of the filename.

2992	    return (
2993	        literal.replace("<former-bc-role>", _PLACEHOLDER_FORMER_BC_ROLE)
2994	        .replace("<new-bc-role>", _PLACEHOLDER_NEW_BC_ROLE)
2995	    )

3388	@then("that file is non-empty")

3451	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
3452	# Then steps — subprocess and import observation
3453	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
3454	
3455	
3456	def _bd_invocations(context: dict) -> list[list[str]]:
3457	    """Return a list of bd-shim invocation argvs from the per-test log,
3458	    or [] if the log doesn't exist (the shim was never invoked)."""
3459	    log = context.get("bd_shim_log")
3460	    if log is None or not log.exists():
3461	        return []
3462	    invocations: list[list[str]] = []
3463	    for line in log.read_text().splitlines():
3464	        if not line.strip():
3465	            continue
3466	        # Format: "<arg1> <arg2> ...|cwd=<cwd>"
3467	        argline = line.split("|cwd=", 1)[0]
3468	        invocations.append(argline.split())
3469	    return invocations
3470	
3471	
3472	@then(
3473	    'during the invocation a subprocess named "bd" was executed with '
3474	    'first argument "init"'
3475	)
3476	def then_bd_init_subprocess_ran(context: dict) -> None:
3477	    invocations = _bd_invocations(context)
3478	    assert invocations, (
3479	        f"expected a `bd` subprocess invocation; bd-shim log is empty"
3480	    )
3481	    first_args = [inv[0] if inv else None for inv in invocations]
3482	    assert "init" in first_args, (
3483	        f"expected `bd init` invocation; got bd args: {first_args!r}"
3484	    )
3485	
3486	
3487	@then(
3488	    'during the invocation no symbol from a Python module whose top-level '
3489	    'package name is "bd" or "beads" was imported into the running '
3490	    '"shop-templates" process'
3491	)
3492	def then_no_bd_or_beads_import(context: dict) -> None:

3515	    # from the subprocess (the only writer that wrote

3517	    # the assertion site.
3518	    real = context["last_invocation_target"]
3519	    marker = real / ".beads" / "__shim_init__"
3520	    assert marker.is_file(), (

3522	        f"the `bd init` subprocess); not present"
3523	    )

3594	        )

3917	    )

3919	def given_existing_git_repo_at_target_no_agents_md_no_settings(

4023	def then_returned_body_contains_substring(needle: str, context: dict) -> None:
4024	    # The "returned body" is whichever template body the most recent
4025	    # When step retrieved through the public template-access surface.
4026	    # That surface is currently exercised by two shop-types of scenario:
4027	    #   1. CLAUDE.md primer access (pre-existing, lead-2gr).
4028	    #   2. .claude/settings.json access (brief 003 scope item A/B/E).
4029	    # Both When steps stash their result under "last_returned_body" so
4030	    # this Then step does not need to know which surface produced the
4031	    # body — only that something did.
4032	    body = context.get("last_returned_body")
4033	    assert body is not None, (
4034	        "no template body has been retrieved this scenario; "
4035	        "the matching When step was not run"
4036	    )
4037	    assert needle in body, (
4038	        f"returned body does not contain literal substring {needle!r}"
4039	    )
4040	
4041	
4042	@then(
4043	    'the returned body contains at least one instruction directing the '
4044	    'reader to run a "bd" subcommand as part of the shop\'s working '
4045	    'discipline'
4046	)
4047	def then_primer_body_directs_bd_subcommand(context: dict) -> None:
4048	    # The scenario requires *at least one instruction directing the
4049	    # reader to run a "bd" subcommand*. We interpret that as: the body
4050	    # mentions a bd subcommand invocation in an instructional context —
4051	    # operationally, the literal substring "bd " (bd followed by a
4052	    # space, i.e. a subcommand follows) appears, AND it appears in a
4053	    # context other than the bare phrase "bd prime" alone (so the
4054	    # body really does direct the reader to a bd subcommand command
4055	    # surface, not just name-drop "bd prime" once).
4056	    body = context["claude_primer_body"]
4057	    # Find every occurrence of "bd <word>" in the body and collect the
4058	    # set of subcommand tokens that follow "bd ".
4059	    import re
4060	    matches = re.findall(r"\bbd ([a-z][a-z\-]*)\b", body)
4061	    assert matches, (
4062	        f"primer body does not contain any 'bd <subcommand>' instruction; "
4063	        f"body:\n{body!r}"
4064	    )
4065	    # At least one *non-empty* subcommand must be present. (re's
4066	    # character class above already requires at least one lowercase
4067	    # letter, so the set is non-empty if matches is non-empty.)
4068	    subcommands = set(matches)
4069	    assert subcommands, (
4070	        "primer body mentions 'bd' but no subcommand follows it"
4071	    )
4072	
4073

4078	# TypeError from str.replace() inside _render_claude_md.
4079	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
4080	
4081	
4082	@when(
4083	    parsers.parse(
4084	        'I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with '
4085	        '--shop-type "{shop_type}" and --target "{alias}" but with no '
4086	        '--shop-name argument'
4087	    )
4088	)
4089	def when_invoke_bootstrap_no_shop_name(
4090	    shop_type: str, alias: str, context: dict, tmp_path: Path
4091	) -> None:
4092	    real = _real_target_for_alias(alias, context)
4093	    result = _run_shop_templates_with_bd_shim(
4094	        [
4095	            "bootstrap",
4096	            "--shop-type",
4097	            shop_type,
4098	            "--target",
4099	            str(real),
4100	        ],
4101	        context,
4102	        tmp_path,
4103	    )
4104	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
4105	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
4106	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
4107	    context["last_invocation_target"] = real
4108	
4109	
4110	@then("the exit code is 2")
4111	def then_exit_code_is_two(context: dict) -> None:
4112	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]
4113	    assert rc == 2, (
4114	        f"expected exit code 2; got {rc}; stderr:\n"
4115	        f"{context.get('cli_stderr', '')}"
4116	    )
4117	
4118	
4119	@then(
4120	    parsers.parse(
4121	        'stderr names "{flag}" as a missing required argument'
4122	    )
4123	)
4124	def then_stderr_names_missing_required_flag(flag: str, context: dict) -> None:
4125	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
4126	    assert flag in stderr, (
4127	        f"expected stderr to name {flag!r} as a missing required argument; "
4128	        f"got:\n{stderr}"
4129	    )
4130	    # "missing required argument" — accept either that exact phrase or
4131	    # the close paraphrase "required argument" / "required: <flag>" so
4132	    # the wording check is concrete but not brittle to phrasing nits.
4133	    stderr_lower = stderr.lower()
4134	    assert (
4135	        "missing required" in stderr_lower
4136	        or "required argument" in stderr_lower
4137	        or "required:" in stderr_lower
4138	        or "required arguments" in stderr_lower
4139	    ), (
4140	        f"expected stderr to identify {flag!r} as a *required* argument; "
4141	        f"got:\n{stderr}"
4142	    )
4143	
4144	
4145	@then(
4146	    'stderr does not contain a Python traceback or the substring '
4147	    '"TypeError"'
4148	)
4149	def then_stderr_has_no_traceback_or_typeerror(context: dict) -> None:
4150	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
4151	    # A Python traceback emitted by the default sys.excepthook always
4152	    # begins with the literal "Traceback (most recent call last):" line.
4153	    # Anything we emit ourselves via print(..., file=sys.stderr) won't
4154	    # contain that header — that's the discriminator the scenario
4155	    # depends on.
4156	    assert "Traceback (most recent call last):" not in stderr, (
4157	        f"unexpected Python traceback header in stderr:\n{stderr}"
4158	    )
4159	    assert "TypeError" not in stderr, (
4160	        f"unexpected 'TypeError' substring in stderr:\n{stderr}"
4161	    )
4162	
4163	
4164	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
4165	# Steps for scenarios fe59a11a88a9ab60 (bootstrap) and 8fe363bd46cb766c
4166	# (update) — CLI rejects missing --target with an argparse-style usage
4167	# error (exit 2, no traceback, no scaffold mutation). The omission of
4168	# --target previously surfaced as a TypeError from Path(args.target).
4169	# Consistency pass adjacent to scenario 3c8612d20608e9a3 (--shop-name).
4170	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
4171	
4172	
4173	def _real_witness_for_alias(alias: str, context: dict, tmp_path: Path) -> Path:
4174	    """Map a feature-file alias to a fresh per-test directory WITHOUT
4175	    git-initializing it.
4176	
4177	    Distinct from `_real_target_for_alias` (which runs `git init` to
4178	    satisfy "an existing git repository at..." Givens). The scenarios
4179	    in this section say "an existing empty directory", so we must NOT
4180	    create a .git/ child — otherwise the witness is no longer empty.
4181	    """
4182	    context.setdefault("bootstrap_workspace", tmp_path)
4183	    mapping = context.setdefault("target_alias_to_real", {})
4184	    if alias in mapping:
4185	        return mapping[alias]
4186	    base = context["bootstrap_workspace"]
4187	    real = base / alias.lstrip("/").replace("/", "_")
4188	    real.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
4189	    mapping[alias] = real
4190	    return real
4191	
4192	
4193	@given(
4194	    parsers.parse(
4195	        'an existing empty directory "{alias}" that contains no '
4196	        '".claude/agents/" directory, no ".beads/" directory, no '
4197	        'top-level "CLAUDE.md", and no top-level ".gitignore"'
4198	    )
4199	)

4339	        if norm.startswith(alias_norm + "/"):

4497	# =======================================================================
4498	# Step definitions — brief 003: event-driven shop activation +
4499	# canonical .claude/settings.json bootstrap extension (lead-1pi).
4500	# =======================================================================
4501	#
4502	# Scope items A/B/C/D/E per the dispatch description. Scenarios:
4503	#
4504	#   - A (57, 68): 1621b59b0ea8b20b — public template-access surface for
4505	#     .claude/settings.json; 287e6a4f31533336 — own/other watch-target
4506	#     exclusivity.
4507	#   - B (58, 65): 679d227f04533ad4 — inotifywait hook shape;
4508	#     a9379ab3a162158d — loud-fail when inotifywait missing.
4509	#   - C (59): c1e7f31eeef73e05 — lead settings.json composes bd prime
4510	#     AND the inotifywait hook as two distinct SessionStart entries.
4511	#   - D (60, 61, 62, 63, 64): f83e03ee69261242 — bootstrap pours the
4512	#     settings.json byte-for-byte; d29cd723439faae1 / d3066d4476d0a975 —
4513	#     update treats settings.json as bootstrap-managed (re-pours stale;
4514	#     leaves byte-equal content untouched); cad1153ef4dfd18c — CLAUDE.md
4515	#     names the host prereqs; 6bc3eb5f62115d91 — the CLAUDE.md primer
4516	#     template body itself names the prereqs (package-data property).
4517	#   - E (66, 67): 3957f255c35aff60 — outcome pin (edits in repos/<bc>/
4518	#     reflect in next CLI invocation without manual reinstall);
4519	#     ff882696856530a4 — mechanism pin (lead-bootstrap installs sibling
4520	#     BC clones editable into product venv).
4521	#
4522	# Convention: tests that need a venv create one at <target>/.venv/
4523	# via shop-templates bootstrap itself (which is the system under test).
4524	
4525

4527	# Given step — target with no .claude/settings.json file
4528	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
4529

4531	@given(

4536	)
4537	def given_existing_git_repo_at_target_no_settings_json(

4540	    context["bootstrap_workspace"] = tmp_path
4541	    real = _real_target_for_alias(alias, context)

4543	        f"premise of Given violated: "
4544	        f"{(real / '.claude' / 'settings.json')!s} unexpectedly exists"
4545	    )

4547	
4548	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
4549	# Given steps — settings.json post-bootstrap state (drives update tests)
4550	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
4551	
4552	
4553	@given(
4554	    parsers.parse(
4555	        'the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory '
4556	        'differs from the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" '
4557	        'template for shop type "{shop_type}"'
4558	    )
4559	)
4560	def given_settings_json_differs_from_canonical(
4561	    shop_type: str, context: dict
4562	) -> None:
4563	    real = _resolve_single_target(context)
4564	    from shop_templates.cli import read_claude_settings_template
4565	
4566	    canonical = read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)
4567	    settings_file = real / ".claude" / "settings.json"
4568	    # Mutate the existing settings.json so its content differs from
4569	    # canonical. We append a JSON-comment-like marker line that breaks
4570	    # JSON equality without making the test brittle to canonical-body
4571	    # formatting (we don't reparse — we just compare bytes).
4572	    if settings_file.exists():
4573	        current = settings_file.read_text()
4574	    else:
4575	        current = ""
4576	    mutated = current + "\n// STALE DRIFT MARKER — not in canonical\n"
4577	    settings_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
4578	    settings_file.write_text(mutated)
4579	    assert settings_file.read_text() != canonical, (
4580	        "premise of Given violated: mutated settings.json equals canonical"
4581	    )
4582	
4583	
4584	@given(
4585	    parsers.parse(
4586	        'the file at ".claude/settings.json" in the target directory '
4587	        'equals the current canonical ".claude/settings.json" template '
4588	        'for shop type "{shop_type}" byte-for-byte'
4589	    )
4590	)
4591	def given_settings_json_equals_canonical(
4592	    shop_type: str, context: dict
4593	) -> None:
4594	    real = _resolve_single_target(context)
4595	    from shop_templates.cli import read_claude_settings_template
4596	
4597	    canonical = read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)
4598	    settings_file = real / ".claude" / "settings.json"
4599	    settings_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

4658	
4659	def _materialize_sibling_bc_clone(
4660	    target: Path, clone_subpath: str, package_name: str, entry_point: str
4661	) -> Path:
4662	    """Create a minimal installable Python package at target/<clone_subpath>
4663	    that ships a console-script named `entry_point`. The script body is
4664	    customized so it emits a uniquely identifying token, used by the
4665	    editable-install + post-edit-reflection scenarios to observe CLI
4666	    behavior changes after editing the source.
4667	    """
4668	    clone_dir = target / clone_subpath.rstrip("/")
4669	    src_dir = clone_dir / "src" / package_name
4670	    src_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
4671	    (src_dir / "__init__.py").write_text("")
4672	    (src_dir / "cli.py").write_text(
4673	        "def main():\n"
4674	        "    print('PRE_EDIT_TOKEN_v1')\n"
4675	    )
4676	    (clone_dir / "pyproject.toml").write_text(
4677	        '[build-system]\n'
4678	        'requires = ["setuptools>=61"]\n'
4679	        'build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"\n\n'
4680	        '[project]\n'
4681	        f'name = "{entry_point}"\n'
4682	        'version = "0.0.1"\n'
4683	        'requires-python = ">=3.10"\n\n'
4684	        '[project.scripts]\n'
4685	        f'{entry_point} = "{package_name}.cli:main"\n\n'
4686	        '[tool.setuptools]\n'
4687	        'package-dir = {"" = "src"}\n\n'
4688	        '[tool.setuptools.packages.find]\n'
4689	        'where = ["src"]\n'
4690	    )
4691	    # Initialize the clone as its own git repository so that "sibling BC
4692	    # clone" matches the lead-shop reality (each BC has its own git
4693	    # remote and history).
4694	    subprocess.run(
4695	        ["git", "init", "-q", str(clone_dir)],
4696	        check=True,
4697	        capture_output=True,
4698	    )
4699	    return clone_dir
4700

4702	@given(

4705	        '"{clone_subpath}" whose installed package name is "{entry_point}"'
4706	    )
4707	)
4708	def given_sibling_bc_clone_with_package_name(
4709	    clone_subpath: str, entry_point: str, context: dict
4710	) -> None:

4712	    # Derive a Python-module-name-safe package name from the entry point
4713	    # (e.g. "shop-msg" -> "shop_msg"). The actual module name does not
4714	    # need to match the entry point name — the scenarios only assert via
4715	    # the entry-point CLI invocation.

4718	        real, clone_subpath, package_name, entry_point
4719	    )
4720	    context.setdefault("sibling_bc_clones", {})[entry_point] = {

4722	        "package_name": package_name,
4723	        "clone_subpath": clone_subpath,
4724	    }

4726	    # clone whose installed package name is <X>" describe a target
4727	    # state in which the clone is part of the lead shop's installed

4730	    # If the Given runs AFTER a previously-bootstrapped Given (so the
