```text
1	# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions
2	
3	This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** lead shop. As an agent operating
4	in this repo, you are operating inside a **lead shop** under the shop-system
5	spec.
6	
7	## Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents
8	
9	By default you are the **router** for this lead shop. The two judgment
10	roles — **PO** and **Architect** per §3 of the shop-system spec — are
11	dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request and
12	delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.
13	
14	The canonical role set for this shop type is:
15	
16	- **lead-po** — authors or sharpens Gherkin scenarios; drafts briefs or
17	  PDRs; responds to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.
18	
19	- **lead-architect** — selects a message-type vehicle; composes
20	  `shop-msg send`; verifies BC pre-state empirically; responds to BC
21	  `clarify` on architecture; reconciles scenario registers; drafts ADRs;
22	  makes BC decomposition decisions.
23	
24	Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` and
25	`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`. These are inline copies of the
26	canonical templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not
27	edit them independently of the canonical source.
28	
29	## How feature requests get handled
30	
31	When a user request implies a new BC capability, a tightening, or a flat
32	change to an existing BC:
33	
34	1. **PO authors intent first.** Brief → PDR → Gherkin scenarios.
35	   Implementation discussion before authored scenarios is the failure
36	   mode §3 exists to prevent.
37	2. **Architect verifies the relevant BC's pre-state empirically.**
38	   Reading the BC's code is hypothesis; running it is fact. Construct a
39	   concrete input that exhibits (or fails to exhibit) the behavior;
40	   observe; cite the demonstration in the dispatch description.
41	3. **Architect applies the message-type discriminator:**
42	   - No capability → `assign_scenarios`.
43	   - Capability exists but unpinned → `request_bugfix`.
44	   - Flat (refactor / doc / value-only) → `request_maintenance`.
45	4. **Architect dispatches via `shop-msg send`.** Never write inbox/outbox
46	   YAML by hand.
47	
48	## Do not
49	
50	- **No implementation code in this repo.** This is the lead shop; code
51	  lives in BCs.
52	- **No skipping the discriminator.** If a request smells like open-ended
53	  "what should we build?" — route it back through the process. First
54	  question: *what scenarios pin this?*
55	
56	## Beads (bd) discipline
57	
58	This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. Lead-shop
59	bead IDs are the canonical `work_id` values that flow outward into
60	`shop-msg send` when dispatching to a BC.
61	
62	- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
63	  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
64	- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
65	  an issue, `bd update <id> --claim` to claim work, and `bd close <id>`
66	  to mark work complete. Do NOT track work in markdown TODO lists or
67	  alternative trackers.
68	- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
69	  session.
70	
71	## Session start: arming the BC-outbox watcher via the in-session Monitor
72	
73	This shop is reactive on session start: when a BC drops a response
74	into one of its outbox directories, the router must learn about it
75	without polling. The activation mechanism is the in-session **Monitor**
76	tool — not a `SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json`. (Earlier
77	iterations of this template tried the hook path; Claude Code awaits
78	`SessionStart` hooks synchronously, so a foreground `inotifywait -m`
79	pipeline never returns and session startup hangs. The Monitor tool is
80	the documented in-session primitive that delivers the
81	streaming-stdout-as-notifications semantic the hook was faking, without
82	blocking startup.)
83	
84	**At session start, the router must arm the in-session Monitor tool on
85	the following pipeline (watch target: `repos/*/outbox/`):**
86	
87	```
88	stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to repos/*/outbox/
89	```
90	
91	Before arming the Monitor, the router must verify that both
92	`inotifywait` and `stdbuf` are on PATH (for example, via
93	`command -v inotifywait` and `command -v stdbuf`). If either executable
94	is missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a
95	visible diagnostic naming the missing prerequisite — do not silently
96	fall back to a no-watcher state, and do not arm the watcher via a
97	`SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` as a fallback. A
98	no-watcher session loses the shop's reactivity invariant; the operator
99	needs to see the diagnostic and install the missing package.
100	
101	The lead shop's canonical `.claude/settings.json` still declares a
102	`SessionStart` hook for `bd prime` (short-lived, returns cleanly — a
103	legitimate synchronous hook usage); the activation hook itself has
104	moved to the router's Monitor invocation described here.
105	
106	### Host prerequisites
107	
108	The Monitor activation pipeline named above depends on two host-level
109	packages being present on PATH:
110	
111	- **inotify-tools** — provides the `inotifywait` binary used to watch
112	  the per-BC outbox directories for new responses.
113	- **coreutils** — provides `stdbuf`, used to set line-buffered output
114	  on the `inotifywait` invocation so events stream as they happen
115	  rather than batching into a pipe buffer.
116	
117	Install both packages through your distro's package manager (e.g.
118	`apt-get install inotify-tools coreutils` on Debian/Ubuntu).
119
```
</details></tool-use>

Now I understand the current CLAUDE.md templates. The new scenarios want:
1. CLAUDE.md body becomes an `@`-import-directive file (not containing `{{SHOP_NAME}}`)
2. Bootstrap writes named shop info to `.claude/shop/name.md`, `.claude/shop/type.md`

5. Update overwrites CLAUDE.md and `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`, but not the shop files

1	"""Shared fixtures and pytest-bdd step definitions for shopsystem-templates.
2	
3	The scenarios under features/ pin the BC's outward contract — the
4	`shop-templates` CLI surface and the structural section headers each
5	role-template markdown file carries. The CLI is invoked via subprocess
6	(the same boundary downstream callers use); content checks read the
7	template via the same CLI subcommand so any drift between the CLI's
8	view of the package data and the file system surfaces here.
9	
10	Style mirrors shopsystem-messaging/tests/conftest.py and
11	shopsystem-scenarios/tests/conftest.py — subprocess + a `context` dict
12	fixture carrying cross-step state.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import re
17	import subprocess
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	import pytest
22	from pytest_bdd import given, parsers, then, when
23	
24	
25	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# Shared cross-step state
27	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	
30	@pytest.fixture
31	def context() -> dict:
32	    return {}
33	
34	
35	def _run_shop_templates(*args: str) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
36	    return subprocess.run(
37	        ["shop-templates", *args],
38	        capture_output=True,
39	        text=True,
40	    )
41	
42	
43	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Given steps
45	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
46	
47	
48	@given(
49	    parsers.parse(
50	        'a template name "{name}" that is in the list output by '
51	        '"shop-templates list"'
52	    )
53	)
54	def given_template_name_in_list(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
55	    # Verify empirically that the name appears in `shop-templates list`
56	    # output — the Given's contract is part of the scenario's premise and
57	    # a future drop of a template would silently undermine the When/Then
58	    # steps if we didn't check it here.
59	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
60	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
61	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
62	    )
63	    listed = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
64	    assert name in listed, (
65	        f"premise of Given violated: {name!r} is not in shop-templates list "
66	        f"output {listed!r}"
67	    )
68	    context["available_templates"] = listed
69	
70	
71	@given(
72	    parsers.parse(
73	        'a template name "{name}" that is not in the list output by '
74	        '"shop-templates list"'
75	    )
76	)
77	def given_template_name_not_in_list(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
78	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
79	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
80	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
81	    )
82	    listed = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
83	    assert name not in listed, (
84	        f"premise of Given violated: {name!r} unexpectedly appears in "
85	        f"shop-templates list output {listed!r}"
86	    )
87	    context["available_templates"] = listed
88	
89	
90	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
91	# When steps
92	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
93	
94	
95	@when(parsers.parse('I run "shop-templates list"'))
96	def when_run_list(context: dict) -> None:
97	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
98	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
99	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
100	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
```

2950	@given(
2951	    'the current canonical role set for shop type "bc" is exactly the '

2955	def given_canonical_role_set_bc(context: dict) -> None:
2956	    # Verify empirically: the canonical role set for "bc" must be a
2957	    # subset of `shop-templates list` output, AND must equal the
2958	    # bootstrap surface's "bc" role set.
2959	    from shop_templates.cli import _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS
2960	
2961	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
2962	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
2963	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
2964	    )
2965	    listed = set(result.stdout.strip().splitlines())
2966	    bc_set = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS["bc"])
2967	    assert bc_set <= listed, (
2968	        f"premise of Given violated: canonical bc set {bc_set!r} not a "
2969	        f"subset of shop-templates list output {listed!r}"
2970	    )
2971	    context["canonical_bc_set"] = bc_set
2972	
2973	
2974	# Bindings for the scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031 placeholders. The
2975	# scenario uses literal "<former-bc-role>.md" and "<new-bc-role>.md"
2976	# tokens (it's a regular Scenario, not a Scenario Outline with
2977	# Examples) — they are placeholder names meant for the test harness
2978	# to bind to concrete values that satisfy the surrounding constraints
2979	# (former NOT in canonical; new IS in canonical). Pick:
2980	#   former-bc-role  = "bc-legacy-deprecated" (NOT in canonical bc set)
2981	#   new-bc-role     = "bc-implementer"        (IS in canonical bc set)
2982	_PLACEHOLDER_FORMER_BC_ROLE = "bc-legacy-deprecated"
2983	_PLACEHOLDER_NEW_BC_ROLE = "bc-implementer"
2984	
2985	
2986	def _bind_role_placeholder(literal: str) -> str:
2987	    """Substitute scenario-level role-name placeholders with the
2988	    concrete values defined above. The literal arrives with the
2989	    angle-bracket markers (e.g. "<former-bc-role>.md"); we replace
2990	    only the bracketed token and keep the rest of the filename.
2991	    """
2992	    return (
2993	        literal.replace("<former-bc-role>", _PLACEHOLDER_FORMER_BC_ROLE)
2994	        .replace("<new-bc-role>", _PLACEHOLDER_NEW_BC_ROLE)
2995	    )

3389	def then_that_file_non_empty(context: dict) -> None:

3391	    # asserted by then_target_contains_top_level_file. We re-read all
3392	    # files in the target's top-level whose names were referenced by
3393	    # the immediately-preceding step. Simpler: scan the target's
3394	    # top-level CLAUDE.md and .gitignore as the only files the
3395	    # scenarios reference with "that file" and pick whichever exists
3396	    # and is non-empty. But that's fragile across scenarios that
3397	    # only check one.
3398	    real = context["last_invocation_target"]
3399	    # The two top-level files written by bootstrap are CLAUDE.md and
3400	    # .gitignore; both scenarios that use "that file is non-empty"

3402	    candidate = context.get("_most_recent_top_level_file")

3404	        # Fallback: check whichever exists.
3405	        for name in ("CLAUDE.md", ".gitignore"):
3406	            p = real / name
3407	            if p.exists():
3408	                assert p.stat().st_size > 0, f"file {p!s} is empty"
3409	                return
3410	        raise AssertionError("no candidate 'that file' identified")
3411	    p = real / candidate
3412	    assert p.stat().st_size > 0, f"file {p!s} is empty"

3455	
3456	def _bd_invocations(context: dict) -> list[list[str]]:
3457	    """Return a list of bd-shim invocation argvs from the per-test log,
3458	    or [] if the log doesn't exist (the shim was never invoked)."""
3459	    log = context.get("bd_shim_log")
3460	    if log is None or not log.exists():
3461	        return []
3462	    invocations: list[list[str]] = []
3463	    for line in log.read_text().splitlines():
3464	        if not line.strip():
3465	            continue
3466	        # Format: "<arg1> <arg2> ...|cwd=<cwd>"
3467	        argline = line.split("|cwd=", 1)[0]
3468	        invocations.append(argline.split())
3469	    return invocations
3470

3475	)

3480	    )

3484	    )

3511	def then_beads_written_by_bd_subprocess(context: dict) -> None:

3676	@then(

3681	def then_agents_dir_matches_canonical_bc(context: dict) -> None:
3682	    real = context["last_invocation_target"]
3683	    agents = real / ".claude" / "agents"
3684	    from shop_templates.cli import _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS
3685	
3686	    # "naming convention" = lowercase hyphenated stem, .md extension.
3687	    # Filter to those candidate filenames and compare set-of-stems.
3688	    stems: set[str] = set()

3691	            continue
3692	        if not p.name.endswith(".md"):
3693	            continue
3694	        stem = p.name[:-3]
3695	        # naming convention: hyphen present, all lowercase, only
3696	        # lowercase letters and hyphens
3697	        if "-" not in stem:
3698	            continue
3699	        if stem != stem.lower():
3700	            continue
3701	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
3702	            continue
3703	        stems.add(stem)
3704	    expected = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS["bc"])

3764	@then(
3765	    parsers.parse(
3766	        'the returned body is the source of truth from which the bootstrap '
3767	        'entry point generates the target directory\'s top-level "CLAUDE.md" '
3768	        'for a shop of type "{shop_type}"'
3769	    )
3770	)
3771	def then_returned_body_is_source_of_truth(
3772	    shop_type: str, context: dict, tmp_path: Path
3773	) -> None:
3774	    # Empirically verify: run bootstrap into a fresh target with shop_name
3775	    # "<source-of-truth-probe>" and check that the resulting CLAUDE.md
3776	    # contains the primer body's invariant content (substituting only
3777	    # the shop-name marker).
3778	    target = tmp_path / f"sot-probe-{shop_type}"
3779	    target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
3780	    subprocess.run(["git", "init", "-q", str(target)], check=True, capture_output=True)
3781	    probe_name = "source-of-truth-probe-shop-name"
3782	    result = _run_shop_templates_with_bd_shim(
3783	        [
3784	            "bootstrap",
3785	            "--shop-type",
3786	            shop_type,
3787	            "--shop-name",
3788	            probe_name,
3789	            "--target",
3790	            str(target),
3791	        ],
3792	        context,
3793	        tmp_path,
3794	    )
3795	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
3796	        f"probe bootstrap failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
3797	    )
3798	    written = (target / "CLAUDE.md").read_text()
3799	    primer_body = context["claude_primer_body"]
3800	    # The primer's invariant content (modulo the {{SHOP_NAME}} marker)
3801	    # must appear in the written CLAUDE.md.
3802	    # Split the primer on the marker and check each chunk is in the
3803	    # written file.
3804	    for chunk in primer_body.split("{{SHOP_NAME}}"):
3805	        if not chunk.strip():
3806	            continue
3807	        assert chunk in written, (
3808	            f"written CLAUDE.md does not contain primer chunk:\n{chunk!r}"
3809	        )
3810	    assert probe_name in written, (
3811	        f"written CLAUDE.md does not contain the substituted shop name "
3812	        f"{probe_name!r}"
3813	    )
3814	
3815	
3816	@then(
3817	    'the returned body is not read from any path under this product\'s '
3818	    'top-level working directory at lookup time'
3819	)
3820	def then_primer_not_from_product_path(context: dict) -> None:
3821	    # Empirically verify: re-do the lookup under a CWD that is NOT
3822	    # under the product working directory, with the product source
3823	    # path removed from sys.path-equivalents, and confirm the same
3824	    # body is returned. The strongest version of this assertion is
3825	    # "the body comes from importlib.resources package data"; we
3826	    # check that by reading it again with importlib.resources and
3827	    # asserting byte-equality.
3828	    import importlib.resources as ires
3829	
3830	    shop_type = context["claude_primer_shop_type"]

4066	    # character class above already requires at least one lowercase

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-19 14:15:45Z)**_

Now I understand the existing infrastructure well. Let me look at the full picture of what the new scenarios need. The new architecture:

1. `read_claude_md_body_template(shop_type)` — returns a template with `@` import lines (NEW public function)
2. Bootstrap writes CLAUDE.md = canonical template body (byte-for-byte), NOT rendered with shop_name
3. Bootstrap creates `.claude/shop/name.md` = shop_name + newline
4. Bootstrap creates `.claude/shop/type.md` = shop_type + newline
5. Bootstrap creates `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md` = canonical primer (read_claude_md_primer)
6. Bootstrap creates `.claude/shop/primer.md` = empty placeholder (no canonical content)
7. Update reads `.claude/shop/type.md` instead of `--shop-type` flag
8. Update overwrites CLAUDE.md with canonical body template
9. Update overwrites `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`
10. Update does NOT touch `.claude/shop/name.md`, `.claude/shop/type.md`, `.claude/shop/primer.md`
11. Update is idempotent (byte+mtime preserved when unchanged)
12. Legacy shop (no `.claude/shop/type.md`) → update exits non-zero with diagnostic

The key question is: what is the "canonical CLAUDE.md body template"? It's a template with `@` import lines referencing the four typed files. This is separate from the "primer" template (the current `claude/bc.md` and `claude/lead.md`). We need a NEW template for the CLAUDE.md body.

Let me verify by re-reading the new scenario `cad9ccb5b462978d` precisely: it asks the package for the "canonical CLAUDE.md body template for shop type X" through its public template-access surface. The body must contain `@` import lines referencing:
- `.claude/shop/name.md`
- `.claude/shop/type.md`
- `.claude/canonical/<shop_type>-primer.md`
- `.claude/shop/primer.md`

And `35c34f0e2d11c092` says the "canonical primer template" is what gets written to `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`. The current `read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)` returns the content of `templates/claude/bc.md` or `templates/claude/lead.md`. So the "canonical CLAUDE.md body template" needs to be a NEW template (e.g., `templates/claude_body/bc.md` or similar) while the "canonical primer template" remains the current `templates/claude/bc.md`.

Wait, let me re-read `35c34f0e2d11c092` more carefully: "the byte contents of that file equal the canonical 'CLAUDE.md' primer template for shop type X byte-for-byte". And `0bba99e6f592a788` says `.claude/shop/primer.md` does NOT contain any non-trivial substring (length 64+) that also appears in the canonical "CLAUDE.md" primer template. So the primer template (the detailed content) goes in `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`.

And the CLAUDE.md body template (scenario `cad9ccb5b462978d`) is a short file with just `@` import directives.

Now let me understand the update contract change:
- `c458502d8632952b`: update DOES overwrite CLAUDE.md with canonical body template (contradicts `56a0ac7107ba5c15`)
- `f55678f733a5427a`: update reads `.claude/shop/type.md` to determine shop type (no --shop-type flag!)
- `e51ac69bba8fd909`: if no `.claude/shop/type.md`, update exits non-zero with diagnostic

This means the `--shop-type` flag for update may become optional or derived from `type.md`. Looking at `f55678f733a5427a` more carefully: "the operator does not pass a shop-type flag to update, and the shop type captured at bootstrap is authoritative for subsequent updates". The When step is: `I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against the target directory "/tmp/example-shop" with no additional shop-type argument`.

This is a significant change to the update contract. Currently the when_invoke_update step passes `--shop-type` explicitly. The new scenarios want update to read from `type.md`. But existing scenarios still need to pass `--shop-type`. Actually, looking at this more carefully: the existing `when_invoke_update` step passes `--shop-type` from `context["bootstrap_shop_type"]`. That's still valid because update can accept `--shop-type` as an override OR fall back to reading `type.md`. But the new scenarios test the case where no `--shop-type` is given.

Actually, the new scenario `f55678f733a5427a` says "with no additional shop-type argument" -- this is a new When step variant.

Let me also check what `e51ac69bba8fd909`'s Given says: "a target directory that contains a top-level CLAUDE.md and has no file at .claude/shop/type.md" -- this is a legacy shop. The When step also uses "the 'shop-templates' update entry point against the target directory" (same pattern as before, so it will need to work without shop-type).

Now let me understand the full scope of changes needed:

**New templates needed:**
1. `templates/claude_body/bc.md` — CLAUDE.md body for bc shops (just `@` import lines)
2. `templates/claude_body/lead.md` — CLAUDE.md body for lead shops (just `@` import lines)
OR reuse the existing path structure as `templates/claude_body/<type>.md`

Actually, let me reconsider naming. The scenarios use "canonical CLAUDE.md body template" — this is distinct from the "canonical CLAUDE.md primer template". The primer goes in `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`. So we need:
- A public function `read_claude_md_body_template(shop_type)` that returns the `@`-import template
- New package data files for the body templates

**New step definitions needed for these scenarios:**
1. `When I ask the "shop-templates" package for the canonical "CLAUDE.md" body template for shop type "<shop_type>" through its public template-access surface`
2. `Then the returned body contains an "@" import line referencing ".claude/shop/name.md"` etc.
3. `Then the byte contents of the file at "CLAUDE.md" in the target directory equal the canonical "CLAUDE.md" body template for shop type "<shop_type>" byte-for-byte`
4. `Given the file at "CLAUDE.md" in the target directory has been edited since bootstrap so that its byte contents are not equal to the canonical "CLAUDE.md" body template`
5. `Then the byte contents of that file are exactly the literal string "<shop_name>" with a single trailing newline and no other content`
6. `Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" with no ".claude/shop/" subdirectory`
7. `Then the target directory contains a file at ".claude/shop/name.md"` (maybe already exists as a general `Then the target directory contains a file at "{path}"`)
8. `When I invoke the "shop-templates" update entry point against the target directory "{alias}" with no additional shop-type argument`
9. `Then after the invocation...` various file content assertions
10. `And I record the byte contents and mtimes of those two files before the invocation` (for idempotency)
11. `And the byte contents of that file do not contain any non-trivial substring (length 64 or greater) that also appears in...`
12. `And I resolve the "@" import directives in the bootstrapped "CLAUDE.md" against the target directory`
13. `And the resolved content contains the byte contents of the file at "..." in the target directory`

Also, the update command needs to change:
- It should read `shop_type` from `.claude/shop/type.md` instead of `--shop-type`
- It should exit non-zero with diagnostic if `.claude/shop/type.md` is missing

- It should overwrite `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md` with canonical primer
- It should NOT touch `.claude/shop/name.md`, `.claude/shop/type.md`, `.claude/shop/primer.md`

And the bootstrap command needs to change:
- Write CLAUDE.md = canonical body template (byte-for-byte, no substitution!)
- Write `.claude/shop/name.md` = shop_name + "\n"
- Write `.claude/shop/type.md` = shop_type + "\n"
- Write `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md` = canonical primer
- Write `.claude/shop/primer.md` = placeholder (empty or minimal, no canonical content)

The scenario `a15dac2f87549b8a` says CLAUDE.md "names the shop's own identity — its shop name and its role set". In the new design, CLAUDE.md is a template with `@` import lines. After `@` resolution, the name.md content would be included (which has the shop name). But the CLAUDE.md file itself would NOT contain the shop name or role names directly — those would be in the referenced files.

However, these existing scenarios are currently PASSING. The new scenarios `2b9bd9c82017b0c6` says CLAUDE.md = canonical body template byte-for-byte. If we change bootstrap to write the body template without substitution, then `a15dac2f87549b8a`'s "content contains the literal substring shop_name" will FAIL.

This is a REAL conflict between existing (active) scenario `a15dac2f87549b8a` and new scenario `2b9bd9c82017b0c6`. I need to determine if `a15dac2f87549b8a` is meant to be retired by this dispatch. Let me check the dispatch message context again... The dispatch context only mentions `56a0ac7107ba5c15` as retired. It doesn't say `a15dac2f87549b8a` is retired.

Similarly scenario `0cce58eb573d3c91` says the primer template is "the source of truth from which bootstrap generates CLAUDE.md" -- but with the new design, the CLAUDE.md body template is different from the primer. The primer now goes to `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`, and CLAUDE.md gets the body template.

Let me also check scenario `0cce58eb573d3c91`'s step `then_returned_body_is_source_of_truth` -- it checks that the primer body appears in the bootstrapped CLAUDE.md. With the new design, the CLAUDE.md body is the `@`-import template (not the primer), so this would fail too.

These are real conflicts. The dispatch says `56a0ac7107ba5c15` is the only hash being retired. But the new scenarios would actually cause several other existing scenarios to fail:
1. `a15dac2f87549b8a` — CLAUDE.md contains shop_name and role names → will fail if CLAUDE.md is just import directives
2. `0cce58eb573d3c91` — primer is source-of-truth for CLAUDE.md → will fail if CLAUDE.md body ≠ primer

This is a conflict I need to surface via `clarify`. The dispatch didn't authorize retirement of `a15dac2f87549b8a` or `0cce58eb573d3c91`, but the new scenarios directly contradict them.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-19 14:17:12Z)**_

Let me look specifically at what happens to existing scenario `0cce58eb573d3c91` -- "source of truth from which bootstrap generates CLAUDE.md". Under the new design, the primer content goes to `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`, not CLAUDE.md. The step `then_returned_body_is_source_of_truth` bootstraps a probe and checks that the primer body chunks appear in CLAUDE.md. This will definitely fail with the new design.

Now let me also verify the `f55678f733a5427a` scenario (update reads type.md) -- the When step uses "with no additional shop-type argument" which is a NEW When step. But let me also check if the existing `when_invoke_update` step (which passes `--shop-type`) would conflict. The existing update scenarios use `when_invoke_update` which passes `--shop-type` from context. The new scenario `f55678f733a5427a` uses a new When step (no shop-type argument). These two can coexist if update supports BOTH: `--shop-type` flag override AND reading from `type.md` as fallback.

But `e51ac69bba8fd909` (legacy shop without `type.md` → exit non-zero) would only fire when no `--shop-type` is passed and no `type.md` exists. The existing step `when_invoke_update` passes `--shop-type`, so existing tests won't hit this path.

However, consider the existing update tests: they pass `--shop-type` AND don't write `type.md`. If I change update to REQUIRE reading `type.md` when no `--shop-type` is given, the existing tests that pass `--shop-type` won't need `type.md`. That's fine.

Actually wait - let me re-read `f55678f733a5427a` more carefully. It says update determines shop type by reading `.claude/shop/type.md` AND "The operator does not pass a shop-type flag to update". The scenario's When step is "I invoke the 'shop-templates' update entry point against the target directory ... with no additional shop-type argument". This is indeed a new When step variant. So I need to add this new step and also modify the update command to support type-less invocation when `type.md` exists.

But the existing update contract (pass `--shop-type`) still works for existing tests. I can support both: if `--shop-type` is passed, use it; otherwise read from `type.md`.

Now let me enumerate the key conflicts I've found:

**Active scenarios that conflict with the new design:**
1. `a15dac2f87549b8a` - CLAUDE.md contains shop_name and role names → incompatible with byte-for-byte body template
2. `0cce58eb573d3c91` - "primer is source of truth for CLAUDE.md" → incompatible with new split (primer goes to `.claude/canonical/`, body template goes to CLAUDE.md)

These are load-bearing conflicts that the dispatch did NOT authorize retiring. I must emit `clarify`.

/workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg respond clarify \
  --bc-root /workspaces/shopsystem-product/repos/shopsystem-templates \
  --work-id lead-2oe \
  --question "The 15 new scenarios (PDR-003 alt F) introduce a CLAUDE.md design incompatible with two currently-active and passing scenarios that the dispatch does not authorize retiring:

1. @scenario_hash:a15dac2f87549b8a (bootstrap_cli_surface.feature:212) — asserts CLAUDE.md 'contains the literal substring <shop_name>' and 'names every role in the canonical role set'. Under the new design, CLAUDE.md is a pure @-import file (body template, byte-for-byte); shop_name goes to .claude/shop/name.md and roles are not named inline. Implementing scenarios 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 and cad9ccb5b462978d would cause a15dac2f87549b8a to fail.

2. @scenario_hash:0cce58eb573d3c91 (bootstrap_cli_surface.feature:200) — asserts the CLAUDE.md primer template body (from read_claude_md_primer()) 'is the source of truth from which the bootstrap entry point generates the target directory's top-level CLAUDE.md'. Under the new design, CLAUDE.md gets the body template (the @-import file) while the primer goes to .claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md. Implementing scenario 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 would cause 0cce58eb573d3c91 to fail because the test checks that primer body chunks appear in CLAUDE.md.

Three-way question for each conflict: (a) retire the old scenario (it is superseded by the new design — please explicitly authorize retirement with the hash in your reply); (b) retire the new scenario (the old invariant still holds — drop the conflicting new scenario); or (c) did the lead mistake the BC's pre-state (the conflicts are already resolved in some way I am not seeing)?"
```

Read shop_msg storage module

```bash
python3 -c "
from shop_msg import storage
import inspect
print(inspect.getsource(storage))
" 2>/dev/null | head -100
```

```text
"""Postgres-backed storage for the shop-msg mailbox protocol.

All inter-shop messages (inbox from lead, outbox from BC) are stored in
a single 'messages' table. The `bc` column namespaces each row to a
specific Bounded Context root (its filesystem path, which is unique per
test invocation and per real BC deployment).

Configuration — connection DSN:
  The DSN is read from the environment variable SHOPMSG_DSN. If that
  variable is not set, a hardcoded default is used (suitable for
  development and the BDD test suite). In production, operators set
  SHOPMSG_DSN to point at the real Postgres instance.

  Default DSN: postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/shopsystem

Schema (DDL emitted once per connect):
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
    id          BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    bc          TEXT NOT NULL,
    work_id     TEXT NOT NULL,
    direction   TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (direction IN ('inbox','outbox')),
    message_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    payload     JSONB NOT NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (bc, work_id, direction, message_type)
  );

Collision handling:
  INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING returns 0 rows affected. The
  callers check this and raise CollisionError, which the CLI surfaces
  as a non-zero exit.

LISTEN/NOTIFY:
  After every inbox INSERT, NOTIFY is fired on the channel
  `inbox_<bc_slug>` (with the bc path slug-encoded) carrying the
  work_id as the payload. This is a fire-and-forget from the storage
  layer's perspective; agents hold a long-lived LISTEN connection
  separately.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import re
from typing import Any

import psycopg
from psycopg.rows import dict_row


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DSN / connection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_DEFAULT_DSN = "postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/shopsystem"


def _get_dsn() -> str:
    """Return the DSN from SHOPMSG_DSN env var or the default."""
    return os.environ.get("SHOPMSG_DSN", _DEFAULT_DSN)


def _connect() -> psycopg.Connection:
    """Open a new connection and ensure the schema exists.

    Raises a clear, descriptive error if the DSN is unreachable so the
    operator knows which endpoint to check.
    """
    dsn = _get_dsn()
    try:
        conn = psycopg.connect(dsn, row_factory=dict_row)
    except psycopg.OperationalError as exc:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"shop-msg: cannot connect to Postgres at DSN {dsn!r}.\n"
            f"Check that the service is running (e.g. 'docker compose up -d').\n"
            f"Original error: {exc}"
        ) from exc
    _ensure_schema(conn)
    return conn


_DDL = """
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
  id           BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  bc           TEXT NOT NULL,
  work_id      TEXT NOT NULL,
  direction    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (direction IN ('inbox','outbox')),
  message_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  payload      JSONB NOT NULL,
  created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE (bc, work_id, direction, message_type)
);
"""


def _ensure_schema(conn: psycopg.Connection) -> None:
    with conn.cursor() as cur:
        cur.execute(_DDL)
    conn.commit()
```
</details></tool-use>

 {"work_id": "lead-2oe", "question": "Three pre-existing BC-side scenarios contradict the new import-graph CLAUDE.md design but are NOT in the lead-mnx retirement authorization list:\n\n1. @scenario_hash:2afb5cba3ea3de25 (features/bootstrap_bd_init_side_effects.feature:29) — \"the top-level CLAUDE.md that bootstrap writes into the target directory references the bd (beads) discipline by naming 'bd prime'\". The new CLAUDE.md body template (per 2b9bd9c82017b0c6) is a pure @-import file; it cannot simultaneously equal the canonical body template byte-for-byte AND contain 'bd prime'. Both pass only if the body template itself contains 'bd prime' — which contradicts the import-graph design intent.\n\n2. @scenario_hash:cad1153ef4dfd18c (features/brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature:42) — \"the top-level CLAUDE.md that bootstrap writes names the host-level prereqs ('inotify-tools', 'coreutils', 'inotifywait', 'stdbuf')\". Same conflict: CLAUDE.md as a pure @-import file cannot contain these substrings.\n\n3. @scenario_hash:9f15982aa00829f1 (features/brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature:134) — \"the top-level CLAUDE.md that bootstrap writes into the target directory contains the router-side Monitor activation instruction for the shop type\". Same conflict: CLAUDE.md as a pure @-import file cannot contain the Monitor instruction.\n\nThese three are analogous to a15dac2f87549b8a (CLAUDE.md contains shop name) which lead-mnx explicitly authorized retiring. My implementation made the BDD suite fail on all three before I reverted.\n\nFor each of the three, please confirm one of:\n  (a) RETIRE — the scenario is superseded by the new design; under PDR-003 alt F, the content moves to .claude/canonical/<shop_type>-primer.md and the bootstrap invariants on CLAUDE.md apply only to the body template surface\n  (b) PRESERVE as-is — the canonical CLAUDE.md body template should embed these strings alongside the @-import lines (would require adding these strings to the body template files)\n  (c) Other action\n\nNote: (b) would mean the body template is NOT a pure import-graph file, which appears to contradict the design spirit of PDR-003 alt F but is technically possible.", "message_type": "clarify"}

```text
  callers check this and raise CollisionError, which the CLI surfaces
  as a non-zero exit.

LISTEN/NOTIFY:
  After every inbox INSERT, NOTIFY is fired on the channel
  `inbox_<bc_slug>` (with the bc path slug-encoded) carrying the
  work_id as the payload. This is a fire-and-forget from the storage
  layer's perspective; agents hold a long-lived LISTEN connection
  separately.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import re
from typing import Any

import psycopg
from psycopg.rows import dict_row


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DSN / connection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_DEFAULT_DSN = "postgresql://postgres:postgres@postgres:5432/shopsystem"


def _get_dsn() -> str:
    """Return the DSN from SHOPMSG_DSN env var or the default."""
    return os.environ.get("SHOPMSG_DSN", _DEFAULT_DSN)
--
class CollisionError(Exception):
    """Raised when an INSERT would violate the UNIQUE constraint."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# bc identifier helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _bc_id(bc_root: str) -> str:
    """Return the bc identifier for a bc_root path.

    The bc column in the messages table is the bc_root path string
    (str(Path(...))). Using the full path keeps each test's tmp_path
    isolated from other tests without any coordination.
    """
    return bc_root


def _bc_slug(bc_root: str) -> str:
    """Return a PostgreSQL identifier-safe slug for use in NOTIFY channel names.

    NOTIFY channel names may not contain certain characters; we replace
    non-alphanumeric characters with underscores and cap length at 63
    bytes (Postgres identifier limit).
    """
    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", bc_root)
    return f"inbox_{slug}"[:63]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
--
    Raises CollisionError if:
    - For inbox direction: any row already exists for (bc, work_id, 'inbox')
      regardless of message_type. A BC receives exactly one message per work_id.
    - For outbox direction: a row with the same (bc, work_id, 'outbox',
      message_type) already exists. The UNIQUE constraint handles this.

    When `notify=True` (inbox inserts), fires NOTIFY after the commit
    so agents listening on the channel wake up.
    """
    bc = _bc_id(bc_root)
    payload_json = json.dumps(payload)
    with _connect() as conn:
        with conn.cursor() as cur:
            # For inbox: enforce work_id uniqueness regardless of message_type.
            # A lead sends exactly one message per work_id into any BC's inbox.
            if direction == "inbox":
                cur.execute(
                    """
                    SELECT 1 FROM messages
                    WHERE bc = %s AND work_id = %s AND direction = 'inbox'
                    LIMIT 1
                    """,
                    (bc, work_id),
                )
                if cur.fetchone() is not None:
                    raise CollisionError(
                        f"inbox message already exists: bc={bc_root!r} "
                        f"work_id={work_id!r}"
                    )

            cur.execute(
                """
                INSERT INTO messages (bc, work_id, direction, message_type, payload)
                VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s::jsonb)
                ON CONFLICT (bc, work_id, direction, message_type) DO NOTHING
                """,
                (bc, work_id, direction, message_type, payload_json),
            )
            rows_affected = cur.rowcount
        conn.commit()

    if rows_affected == 0:
        raise CollisionError(
            f"message already exists: bc={bc_root!r} work_id={work_id!r} "
            f"direction={direction!r} message_type={message_type!r}"
        )

    if notify and direction == "inbox":
        # Fire NOTIFY on a separate autocommit connection so the
        # notification is delivered even if the caller does not hold the
        # connection open. The channel name is the bc slug; the payload
        # is just the work_id (stays well under the 8KB NOTIFY cap).
        # NOTIFY requires a literal channel name (identifier) — it does
        # not accept parameterized channel names — so we format it as a
        # quoted identifier using psycopg's sql.Identifier to prevent
        # injection while keeping the syntax valid.
        from psycopg import sql
        channel = _bc_slug(bc_root)
        with psycopg.connect(_get_dsn(), autocommit=True) as nconn:
            nconn.execute(
                sql.SQL("NOTIFY {channel}, {payload}").format(
                    channel=sql.Identifier(channel),
                    payload=sql.Literal(work_id),
                )
            )


def query_pending_inbox(bc_root: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Return (work_id, message_type) pairs for unprocessed inbox messages.

    A message is 'pending' iff its inbox row has no corresponding outbox
    row for the same (bc, work_id). The query mirrors the directory-glob
    approach the file-based backend used.
    """
--
    # dict already (the binary format deserializes it).
    if isinstance(payload, str):
        return json.loads(payload)
    return payload


def read_outbox_messages(bc_root: str, work_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Return all outbox payload dicts for a work_id, newest last."""
    bc = _bc_id(bc_root)
    with _connect() as conn:
        with conn.cursor() as cur:
            cur.execute(
                """
                SELECT payload, message_type FROM messages
                WHERE bc = %s AND work_id = %s AND direction = 'outbox'
                ORDER BY created_at
                """,
                (bc, work_id),
            )
            rows = cur.fetchall()
    result = []
    for row in rows:
        payload = row["payload"]
        if isinstance(payload, str):
            payload = json.loads(payload)
        result.append(payload)
    return result


def outbox_row_exists(bc_root: str, work_id: str, message_type: str) -> bool:
    """Return True iff an outbox row already exists for this bc/work_id/message_type."""
    bc = _bc_id(bc_root)
    with _connect() as conn:
        with conn.cursor() as cur:
            cur.execute(
                """
                SELECT 1 FROM messages
                WHERE bc = %s AND work_id = %s
                  AND direction = 'outbox' AND message_type = %s
                LIMIT 1
                """,
                (bc, work_id, message_type),
            )
            return cur.fetchone() is not None


def inbox_row_exists(bc_root: str, work_id: str) -> bool:
    """Return True iff any inbox row exists for this bc/work_id."""
    bc = _bc_id(bc_root)
    with _connect() as conn:
        with conn.cursor() as cur:
            cur.execute(
                """
                SELECT 1 FROM messages
                WHERE bc = %s AND work_id = %s AND direction = 'inbox'
                LIMIT 1
                """,
                (bc, work_id),
            )
            return cur.fetchone() is not None

--
    Does NOT fire NOTIFY. Raises CollisionError on duplicate.
    """
    insert_message(bc_root, work_id, direction, message_type, payload, notify=False)
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
=== _cmd_respond_clarify ===
def _cmd_respond_clarify(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    bc_root = str(Path(args.bc_root).resolve())

    if not args.question:
        print(
            "shop-msg respond clarify: question must not be empty",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1

    if not args.work_id or "/" in args.work_id or ".." in args.work_id:
        print(
            "shop-msg respond clarify: refusing unsafe work_id",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1

    message = Clarify(
        message_type="clarify",
        work_id=args.work_id,
        question=args.question,
    )

    try:
        insert_message(
            bc_root,
            args.work_id,
            "outbox",
            "clarify",
            message.model_dump(),
        )
    except CollisionError:
        print(
            f"shop-msg respond clarify: refusing to overwrite existing outbox "
            f"entry for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1
    return 0


=== _cmd_respond_mechanism_observation ===
def _cmd_respond_mechanism_observation(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    bc_root = str(Path(args.bc_root).resolve())

    # Path-safety: refuse work_ids that would escape the outbox dir.
    if "/" in args.work_id or ".." in args.work_id or not args.work_id:
        print(
            f"shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: refusing unsafe "
            f"work_id {args.work_id!r}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1

    message = MechanismObservation(
        message_type="mechanism_observation",
        subject=args.subject,
        observed_during=args.observed_during,
        body=args.body,
        evidence=list(args.evidence) if args.evidence else None,
        proposed_action=args.proposed_action,
        provenance_ref=args.provenance_ref,
    )

    try:
        insert_message(
            bc_root,
            args.work_id,
            "outbox",
            "mechanism_observation",
            message.model_dump(exclude_none=True),
        )
    except CollisionError:
        print(
            f"shop-msg respond mechanism_observation: refusing to overwrite "
            f"existing outbox entry for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1
    return 0


=== _cmd_respond_work_done ===
def _cmd_respond_work_done(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    bc_root = str(Path(args.bc_root).resolve())

    message = WorkDone(
        message_type="work_done",
        work_id=args.work_id,
        status=args.status,
        summary=args.summary,
        scenario_hashes=list(args.scenario_hash or []),
    )

    try:
        insert_message(
            bc_root,
            args.work_id,
            "outbox",
            "work_done",
            message.model_dump(),
        )
    except CollisionError:
        print(
            f"shop-msg respond work_done: refusing to overwrite existing outbox "
            f"entry for work_id={args.work_id!r}",
            file=sys.stderr,
        )
        return 1
    return 0
```
</details></tool-use>

## What was observed

The prior clarify outbox entry for lead-2oe (which asked about hashes 2afb5cba3ea3de25, cad1153ef4dfd18c, 9f15982aa00829f1) remains in the database. Those three have since been retired. A new dispatch attempt was made by the router; I applied the sufficiency check fresh and found TWO ADDITIONAL conflicts that were NOT covered by the prior clarify:

### Conflict 1: a15dac2f87549b8a (bootstrap_cli_surface.feature:212)

Active scenario: "CLAUDE.md contains the literal substring <shop_name> and names every role in the canonical role set".

This conflicts directly with new scenario 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 ("CLAUDE.md equals the canonical body template byte-for-byte, with NO shop-specific splicing"). Since the Examples table uses shop_name="shopsystem-messaging" and "shopsystem-product" and the canonical body template is the same for both runs, CLAUDE.md cannot simultaneously be byte-for-byte canonical AND contain the specific shop name.

### Conflict 2: 0cce58eb573d3c91 (bootstrap_cli_surface.feature:200)

Active scenario: "the primer template body (from read_claude_md_primer()) is the source of truth from which bootstrap generates CLAUDE.md". The step then_returned_body_is_source_of_truth bootstraps a probe shop and checks that primer chunks appear in CLAUDE.md.

Under the new design, CLAUDE.md gets the body template (@-import file) and the primer goes to .claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md. The primer content will NOT appear in CLAUDE.md anymore.

## Why this is load-bearing

Both scenarios are CURRENTLY PASSING (part of the 128 passing). Implementing lead-2oe as written would regress them. The contract requires all scenarios to pass.

## Why clarify is blocked

The outbox for lead-2oe already has a clarify entry (sent by the prior implementer dispatch). The shop-msg CLI refuses to emit a second clarify for the same (bc, work_id, direction, message_type) tuple.

## Proposed action

The lead shop needs to explicitly authorize retirement of both a15dac2f87549b8a and 0cce58eb573d3c91 (or issue a new work_id that carries that authorization). Because clarify is blocked for lead-2oe, the lead must either: (a) send a new inbox message with the authorization, or (b) the lead architect needs to directly update the retirement list and re-dispatch with a new work_id that the implementer can respond to fresh.
EOF
)" \
  --observed-during lead-2oe

Conflicts with new scenario 2b9bd9c82017b0c6 ("CLAUDE.md equals body template byte-for-byte, NO shop-specific splicing"). Active scenario: "the primer template (read_claude_md_primer()) is the source of truth for CLAUDE.md". The test step bootstraps a probe and checks that primer body chunks appear in CLAUDE.md. Under the new design, CLAUDE.md gets the @-import body template; primer goes to .claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md. Primer content will NOT appear in CLAUDE.md.

## Why this is load-bearing

Both are in the current 128-passing suite. Implementing lead-2oe would regress them.

## Why clarify is blocked

Outbox for lead-2oe already has a clarify row (message_type='clarify'). The CLI refuses a second clarify for the same (bc, work_id, direction, message_type).

## Required action

Lead shop must explicitly authorize retirement of a15dac2f87549b8a and 0cce58eb573d3c91, and issue a new work_id (or new inbox row) so the implementer can respond fresh.
EOF
)" \
  --observed-during lead-2oe

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-19 14:20:07Z)**_

The mechanism_observation was recorded. Now let me provide my final report.

**Summary of what happened:**

**Response type emitted:** `mechanism_observation` (the only response I could emit — `clarify` was blocked by a prior clarify response in the outbox; work_done is inappropriate since the work is not done).

**Sufficiency check result:** FAIL — the dispatch is NOT sufficient to implement because two actively-passing scenarios are unretired and directly conflict with the new design:

1. `a15dac2f87549b8a` (bootstrap_cli_surface.feature:212) — asserts CLAUDE.md "contains the literal substring `<shop_name>`" and "names every role in the canonical role set". This is incompatible with new scenario `2b9bd9c82017b0c6` which says CLAUDE.md equals the canonical body template byte-for-byte with no shop-specific splicing. A canonical body template cannot simultaneously contain both "shopsystem-messaging" and "shopsystem-product".

2. `0cce58eb573d3c91` (bootstrap_cli_surface.feature:200) — asserts "the primer template body is the source of truth from which bootstrap generates CLAUDE.md". The test (`then_returned_body_is_source_of_truth`) bootstraps a probe and checks that primer body chunks appear in CLAUDE.md. Under the new design, CLAUDE.md gets the `@`-import body template; the primer goes to `.claude/canonical/<type>-primer.md`. The primer content will NOT appear in CLAUDE.md.

**Why I could not emit `clarify`:** The outbox for `lead-2oe` already contains a `clarify` row (written by the prior dispatch, asking about the now-retired hashes `2afb5cba3ea3de25`, `cad1153ef4dfd18c`, `9f15982aa00829f1`). The `shop-msg` CLI enforces a unique constraint on `(bc, work_id, direction, message_type)` and refuses to overwrite.

**Fields of the inbox message used:** `work_id`, `scenarios[].hash` (all 15 hashes), `scenarios[].gherkin` (all scenario text to compare against existing feature files).

**Action required by lead shop:** Authorize explicit retirement of `a15dac2f87549b8a` and `0cce58eb573d3c91` (they are superseded by the PDR-003 alt F design), then issue a new inbox message or new work_id so the implementer can respond fresh to an unblocked dispatch.

| `0cce58eb573d3c91` | same file, L200 | primer body is source of truth for CLAUDE.md — incompatible with new `@`-import design |

    @\" import line referencing \".claude/canonical/<shop_type>-primer.md\"\n  And\
    \ the returned body contains an \"@\" import line referencing \".claude/shop/primer.md\"\
    \n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type |\n    | bc        |\n    | lead      |\n\n"
- hash: 2b9bd9c82017b0c6
  tags:
  - '@scenario_hash:2b9bd9c82017b0c6'
  - '@bc:shopsystem-templates'
  gherkin: "Feature: import-graph CLAUDE.md: typed files, bootstrap, and update contracts\
    \ (PDR-003 alt F)\n\n  @scenario_hash:2b9bd9c82017b0c6 @bc:shopsystem-templates\n\
    \  Scenario Outline: bootstrap writes the top-level \"CLAUDE.md\" with body byte-for-byte\
    \ equal to the canonical \"CLAUDE.md\" body template for the chosen shop type,\
    \ with no shop-specific splicing into the file itself\n  Given an existing git\
    \ repository at a target directory \"<target>\" with no top-level \"CLAUDE.md\"\
    \n  When I invoke the \"shop-templates\" bootstrap entry point with shop type\
    \ \"<shop_type>\", shop name \"<shop_name>\", and target directory \"<target>\"\
    \n  Then the exit code is 0\n  And the target directory contains a top-level file\
    \ named \"CLAUDE.md\"\n  And the byte contents of the file at \"CLAUDE.md\" in\
    \ the target directory equal the canonical \"CLAUDE.md\" body template for shop\
    \ type \"<shop_type>\" byte-for-byte\n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name\
    \               | target                       |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging\
    \    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |\n    | lead      | shopsystem-product  \
    \    | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |\n\n"

- hash: 0bba99e6f592a788

  - '@bc:shopsystem-templates'
  gherkin: "Feature: import-graph CLAUDE.md: typed files, bootstrap, and update contracts\
    \ (PDR-003 alt F)\n\n  @scenario_hash:0bba99e6f592a788 @bc:shopsystem-templates\n\
    \  Scenario Outline: bootstrap writes \".claude/shop/primer.md\" into the target\
    \ directory as a shop-authored placeholder file that the operator may populate\
    \ later; the file exists after bootstrap and contains no canonical primer text\n\
    \  Given an existing git repository at a target directory \"<target>\" with no\
    \ \".claude/shop/\" subdirectory\n  When I invoke the \"shop-templates\" bootstrap\
    \ entry point with shop type \"<shop_type>\", shop name \"<shop_name>\", and target\
    \ directory \"<target>\"\n  Then the exit code is 0\n  And the target directory\
    \ contains a file at \".claude/shop/primer.md\"\n  And the byte contents of that\
    \ file do not contain any non-trivial substring (length 64 or greater) that also\
    \ appears in the canonical \"CLAUDE.md\" primer template for shop type \"<shop_type>\"\
    \n\n  Examples:\n    | shop_type | shop_name               | target          \
    \             |\n    | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop\

213	  Scenario Outline: the top-level "CLAUDE.md" that bootstrap writes into the target directory names the shop's own identity — its shop name and its role set — and is consistent with the canonical primer template for the chosen shop type
214	    Given an existing git repository at a target directory "<target>" with no top-level "CLAUDE.md"
215	    When I invoke the "shop-templates" bootstrap entry point with shop type "<shop_type>", shop name "<shop_name>", and target directory "<target>"
216	    Then the exit code is 0
217	    And the target directory contains a top-level file named "CLAUDE.md"
218	    And the content of that file contains the literal substring "<shop_name>"
219	    And the content of that file names every role in the canonical role set for shop type "<shop_type>" by name
220	    And the content of that file does not name any role from the canonical role set of the other shop type
221	
222	    Examples:
223	      | shop_type | shop_name               | target                       |
224	      | bc        | shopsystem-messaging    | /tmp/example-bc-shop         |
225	      | lead      | shopsystem-product      | /tmp/example-lead-shop       |
226
```
