 `./src/shop_templates/cli.py`

```text
1	"""shop-templates CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	    list
5	        Print available role-template names (one per line) to stdout.
6	    show <name>
7	        Print the named template's content to stdout. Exits non-zero
8	        with a stderr message when no template matches that name.
9	    bootstrap --shop-type {bc,lead} --shop-name NAME --target DIR
10	        Scaffold a new (existing-but-empty) shop repository at DIR:
11	        write the canonical role-prompt copies for the shop type under
12	        .claude/agents/, write a CLAUDE.md primer for the shop type,
13	        write a top-level .gitignore, and invoke `bd init` as a
14	        subprocess to initialize .beads/.
15	    update --target DIR --shop-type {bc,lead}
16	        Re-pour the bootstrap-managed agent files in DIR/.claude/agents/
17	        from the current canonical package data. Reconcile against the
18	        canonical role set for the shop type — add canonical files that
19	        are missing, remove managed files whose canonical template no
20	        longer exists. Does NOT touch CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or .beads/.
21	
22	Role-prompt templates live in the package as `templates/<name>.md` data
23	files. CLAUDE.md primer templates live as `templates/claude/<shop_type>.md`.
24	The .gitignore template lives as `templates/gitignore.template`. The CLI
25	is the public boundary; dispatchers, scaffold scripts, and any other
26	consumer reads templates via this surface rather than by path.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import argparse
31	import subprocess
32	import sys
33	from importlib.resources import files
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	
37	_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates"
38	_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude"
39	_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG = "shop_templates.templates.claude_settings"
40	
41	# The canonical role-set assignment per shop type. The bootstrap surface
42	# uses this to decide which role files to pour into .claude/agents/ for
43	# a freshly-bootstrapped shop, and the update surface uses it to
44	# reconcile an existing shop's .claude/agents/ against the current
45	# canonical set.
46	_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
47	    "bc": ("bc-implementer", "bc-reviewer"),
48	    "lead": ("lead-po", "lead-architect"),
49	}
50	
51	_SHOP_TYPES = tuple(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.keys())
52	
53	
54	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
55	# Public template-access surface (importable; not just CLI-internal).
56	#
57	# Per scenario 0cce58eb573d3c91, the CLAUDE.md primer template must be
58	# accessible "through its public template-access surface" and must NOT
59	# be read from any path under the product's top-level working directory
60	# at lookup time — i.e., it must be served from importlib.resources
61	# package data, the same boundary the role-prompt templates use.
62	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	
65	def _list_template_names() -> list[str]:
66	    """Return the sorted list of available role-template names.
67	
68	    Walks only the top-level package directory; sub-directories (e.g.
69	    `templates/claude/`) are skipped. This keeps `shop-templates list`
70	    output stable as new categories of package data (CLAUDE primers,
71	    gitignore template) are added.
72	    """
73	    pkg_root = files(_TEMPLATES_PKG)
74	    names = []
75	    for resource in pkg_root.iterdir():
76	        # `is_file()` plus `.md` suffix isolates role-prompt templates
77	        # from sub-directories like `templates/claude/`.
78	        if not resource.is_file():
79	            continue
80	        name = resource.name
81	        if name.endswith(".md"):
82	            names.append(name[: -len(".md")])
83	    return sorted(names)
84	
85	
86	def _read_template(name: str) -> str | None:
87	    """Return the named role-template's content, or None if no such template."""
88	    try:
89	        return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{name}.md").read_text()
90	    except FileNotFoundError:
91	        return None
92	
93	
94	def read_claude_md_primer(shop_type: str) -> str:
95	    """Return the canonical CLAUDE.md primer template body for a shop type.
96	
97	    The body is loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is
98	    never read from a filesystem path under the product working
99	    directory. The returned string is the source of truth from which the
100	    bootstrap entry point generates a target directory's top-level
101	    CLAUDE.md.
102	    """
103	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
104	        raise ValueError(
105	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
106	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
107	        )
108	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_TEMPLATES_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.md"
109	    return resource.read_text()
110	
111	
112	def read_gitignore_template() -> str:
113	    """Return the canonical top-level .gitignore template body."""
114	    return (files(_TEMPLATES_PKG) / "gitignore.template").read_text()
115	
116	
117	def read_claude_settings_template(shop_type: str) -> str:
118	    """Return the canonical ".claude/settings.json" template body for a shop type.
119	
120	    Per scenarios 1621b59b0ea8b20b and family (brief 003 §A): the body is
121	    loaded from package data via importlib.resources; it is never read
122	    from a filesystem path under the product working directory. The
123	    returned string is the source of truth from which the bootstrap
124	    entry point generates a target directory's ".claude/settings.json"
125	    for a shop of the given type, and is also what the update entry
126	    point re-pours.
127	
128	    Per scenarios 679d227f04533ad4, c1e7f31eeef73e05, and 287e6a4f31533336:
129	    the body's content discipline (SessionStart hook shape, watch
130	    target per shop type, the `bd prime` companion hook on lead,
131	    alias-bypassing grep, exclusion of the other shop type's watch
132	    target) is a property of the package-data file itself — this
133	    accessor is a pure pass-through.
134	    """
135	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
136	        raise ValueError(
137	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
138	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
139	        )
140	    resource = files(_CLAUDE_SETTINGS_PKG) / f"{shop_type}.json"
141	    return resource.read_text()
142	
143	
144	def canonical_role_set(shop_type: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
145	    """Return the canonical role-prompt-template names for a shop type.
146	
147	    The bootstrap surface treats these names as the canonical role set
148	    for the given shop type — both `bootstrap` (initial pour) and
149	    `update` (reconciliation) work from this mapping.
150	    """
151	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
152	        raise ValueError(
153	            f"unknown shop type {shop_type!r}; accepted values: "
154	            f"{', '.join(_SHOP_TYPES)}"
155	        )
156	    return _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]
157	
158	
159	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
160	# `list` / `show` subcommands — pre-existing surface.
161	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
162	
163	
164	def _cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
165	    for name in _list_template_names():
166	        print(name)
167	    return 0
168	
169	
170	def _cmd_show(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
171	    content = _read_template(args.name)
172	    if content is None:
173	        available = ", ".join(_list_template_names())
174	        print(
175	            f"shop-templates show: no template named {args.name!r}. Available: {available}",
176	            file=sys.stderr,
177	        )
178	        return 1
179	    # Use sys.stdout.write to preserve exact bytes (no trailing newline from print).
180	    sys.stdout.write(content)
181	    return 0
182	
183	
184	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
185	# `bootstrap` subcommand
186	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
187	
188	
189	def _render_claude_md(shop_type: str, shop_name: str) -> str:
190	    """Render the CLAUDE.md primer for a shop type by substituting the
191	    shop name. The primer must name the shop's identity (its shop_name
192	    and its role set) per scenario a15dac2f87549b8a.
193	    """
194	    primer = read_claude_md_primer(shop_type)
195	    return primer.replace("{{SHOP_NAME}}", shop_name)
196	
197	
198	def _bd_init_in(target: Path) -> int:
199	    """Invoke `bd init` as a subprocess in the target directory.
200	
201	    Per scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e, .beads/ MUST be initialized by a
202	    subprocess named "bd" with first argument "init", NOT by importing
203	    bd / beads internals into the running shop-templates process and
204	    NOT by writing .beads/ files from shop-templates directly. This
205	    function is the single place where that subprocess is spawned.
206	
207	    Per scenario 32d99f6d4a2dad37 (lead-2gr round 2 of lead-k8v's
208	    bd-init side-effect closure), `bd init` MUST be invoked with the
209	    `--skip-agents` flag. That flag is bd's own opt-out for the
210	    AGENTS.md / .claude/settings.json / CLAUDE.md-beads-block append
211	    that bd otherwise applies during `bd init`. The shop's canonical
212	    agent surface is .claude/agents/*.md alone (poured by bootstrap
213	    itself); bd's agent-surface generation is duplicative at best and
214	    contradictory at worst, so we suppress it. Verified against bd
215	    1.0.3: `BD_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 bd init --skip-agents --quiet` in a
216	    fresh probe dir produces .beads/ + .gitignore and no AGENTS.md,
217	    no .claude/.
218	    """
219	    result = subprocess.run(
220	        ["bd", "init", "--skip-agents"],
221	        cwd=str(target),
222	        capture_output=True,
223	        text=True,
224	    )
225	    if result.returncode != 0:
226	        print(
227	            f"shop-templates bootstrap: `bd init` failed in {target!s} "
228	            f"(exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n{result.stderr}",
229	            file=sys.stderr,
230	        )
231	    return result.returncode
232	
233	
234	def _validate_shop_type(shop_type: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
235	    """Return None if shop_type is valid; else print a usage diagnostic to
236	    stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
237	
238	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios d6e935bca62d0039 (missing
239	    shop type) and 7941cdc591cd2c3b (invalid shop type): stderr names
240	    the offending input AND lists the accepted values "bc" and "lead".
241	    """
242	    accepted = ", ".join(f'"{s}"' for s in _SHOP_TYPES)
243	    if shop_type is None:
244	        print(
245	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: shop type. "
246	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
247	            file=sys.stderr,
248	        )
249	        return "missing"
250	    if shop_type not in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS:
251	        print(
252	            f"shop-templates {command}: invalid shop type {shop_type!r}. "
253	            f"Accepted values: {accepted}.",
254	            file=sys.stderr,
255	        )
256	        return "invalid"
257	    return None
258	
259	
260	def _validate_shop_name(shop_name: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
261	    """Return None if shop_name is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
262	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
263	
264	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenario 3c8612d20608e9a3: stderr
265	    names "--shop-name" as the missing required argument and emits no
266	    Python traceback / no "TypeError" substring. We validate manually
267	    (matching the in-house pattern used by `_validate_shop_type`) rather
268	    than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is ours, not
269	    argparse's auto-generated message.
270	    """
271	    if shop_name is None:
272	        print(
273	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --shop-name. "
274	            f"Provide the shop's own name (e.g. shopsystem-messaging).",
275	            file=sys.stderr,
276	        )
277	        return "missing"
278	    return None
279	
280	
281	def _validate_target(target: str | None, command: str) -> str | None:
282	    """Return None if target is provided; else print a usage diagnostic
283	    to stderr and return an error sentinel string usable by callers.
284	
285	    The diagnostic shape is pinned by scenarios fe59a11a88a9ab60
286	    (bootstrap) and 8fe363bd46cb766c (update): stderr names "--target"
287	    as the missing required argument and emits no Python traceback / no
288	    "TypeError" substring. We validate manually (matching the in-house
289	    pattern used by `_validate_shop_type` and `_validate_shop_name`)
290	    rather than letting argparse short-circuit so the error shape is
291	    ours, not argparse's auto-generated message — and so the
292	    diagnostic surfaces BEFORE any Path(args.target) construction that
293	    would otherwise raise TypeError when target is None.
294	    """
295	    if target is None:
296	        print(
297	            f"shop-templates {command}: missing required argument: --target. "
298	            f"Provide the path to the target repository.",
299	            file=sys.stderr,
300	        )
301	        return "missing"
302	    return None
303	
304	
305	def _cmd_bootstrap(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
306	    # Validate shop_type early so we can emit the usage diagnostic shape
307	    # the scenarios pin (named offending input + list of accepted values)
308	    # BEFORE touching the target directory. Scenarios d6e935bca62d0039
309	    # and 7941cdc591cd2c3b both require "writes no scaffold" when the
310	    # shop type is bad.
311	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "bootstrap")
312	    if err is not None:
313	        return 2
314	
315	    # Validate shop_name early for the same reason. Scenario
316	    # 3c8612d20608e9a3 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--shop-name" + no
317	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --shop-name is omitted.
318	    # Without this guard, args.shop_name=None propagates into
319	    # _render_claude_md and surfaces as a TypeError from str.replace().
320	    err = _validate_shop_name(args.shop_name, "bootstrap")
321	    if err is not None:
322	        return 2
323	
324	    # Validate target early for the same reason. Scenario
325	    # fe59a11a88a9ab60 pins exit 2 + stderr names "--target" + no
326	    # traceback + no scaffold written when --target is omitted. Without
327	    # this guard, args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces
328	    # as a TypeError from pathlib.
329	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "bootstrap")
330	    if err is not None:
331	        return 2
332	
333	    target = Path(args.target)
334	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
335	    shop_name: str = args.shop_name
336	
337	    # Pour role-prompt files into .claude/agents/.
338	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
339	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
340	    for role_name in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type]:
341	        body = _read_template(role_name)
342	        if body is None:
343	            print(
344	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: internal error — canonical "
345	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
346	                file=sys.stderr,
347	            )
348	            return 1
349	        (agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md").write_text(body)
350	
351	    # Top-level CLAUDE.md — render the primer with the shop's identity.
352	    (target / "CLAUDE.md").write_text(_render_claude_md(shop_type, shop_name))
353	
354	    # Top-level .gitignore — pour the canonical template.
355	    (target / ".gitignore").write_text(read_gitignore_template())
356	
357	    # .claude/settings.json — pour the canonical per-shop-type settings
358	    # template (SessionStart activation hook over the shop's inbound
359	    # mailbox surface). Per brief 003 scope item A (scenarios
360	    # 1621b59b0ea8b20b, c8002527857e0dd1, f83e03ee69261242): the
361	    # canonical settings template lives in package data and bootstrap
362	    # writes it into the target's .claude/settings.json byte-for-byte.
363	    claude_dir = target / ".claude"
364	    claude_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
365	    (claude_dir / "settings.json").write_text(
366	        read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)
367	    )
368	
369	    # Initialize .beads/ via a `bd init` subprocess. shop-templates MUST
370	    # NOT import bd / beads internals and MUST NOT write to .beads/
371	    # directly (scenario 0c6f1c5d9bc4226e).
372	    rc = _bd_init_in(target)
373	    if rc != 0:
374	        return rc
375	
376	    # Lead-shop bootstrap installs each sibling BC clone under repos/
377	    # editable into the product venv, per brief 003 scope item E
378	    # (scenarios 3957f255c35aff60 outcome pin / ff882696856530a4
379	    # mechanism pin). The reference realization: for each subdir of
380	    # target/repos/ that ships a Python package (pyproject.toml or
381	    # setup.py), pip-install it editable into target/.venv/.
382	    if shop_type == "lead":
383	        rc = _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable(target)
384	        if rc != 0:
385	            return rc
386	
387	    return 0
388	
389	
390	def _install_sibling_bc_clones_editable(target: Path) -> int:
391	    """For a lead shop, install every sibling BC clone under target/repos/
392	    editable into target/.venv/.
393	
394	    Per brief 003 scope item E (scenarios 3957f255c35aff60 /
395	    ff882696856530a4): on session start in a bootstrapped lead shop,
396	    every BC CLI installed into the product venv must resolve invocations
397	    to the current source tree of that BC's clone under repos/, so a
398	    source edit in a BC clone is reflected in the next CLI invocation
399	    without an intervening manual reinstall. The reference realization
400	    is `pip install -e` per BC-clone subdir.
401	
402	    Well-defined no-op states (returns 0 silently):
403	      - target/repos/ does not exist (lead-shop bootstrap with no BC
404	        clones yet — venv creation is also a no-op here).
405	      - target/repos/ exists but contains no installable subdir.
406	
407	    If at least one installable subdir is present, ensure the venv
408	    exists (creating it on demand) and install each installable subdir
409	    in editable mode.
410	    """
411	    repos_dir = target / "repos"
412	    if not repos_dir.exists() or not repos_dir.is_dir():
413	        return 0
414	
415	    # Filter to installable subdirs first; defer venv creation until
416	    # we know we have at least one clone to install.
417	    installable_children = []
418	    for child in sorted(repos_dir.iterdir()):
419	        if not child.is_dir():
420	            continue
421	        if (
422	            (child / "pyproject.toml").exists()
423	            or (child / "setup.py").exists()
424	            or (child / "setup.cfg").exists()
425	        ):
426	            installable_children.append(child)
427	
428	    if not installable_children:
429	        return 0
430	
431	    venv_dir = target / ".venv"
432	    if not venv_dir.exists():
433	        result = subprocess.run(
434	            [sys.executable, "-m", "venv", str(venv_dir)],
435	            capture_output=True,
436	            text=True,
437	        )
438	        if result.returncode != 0:
439	            print(
440	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: failed to create venv at "
441	                f"{venv_dir!s} (exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n"
442	                f"{result.stderr}",
443	                file=sys.stderr,
444	            )
445	            return result.returncode
446	
447	    venv_python = venv_dir / "bin" / "python"
448	    if not venv_python.exists():
449	        # Windows or other layouts — fall back to Scripts/python.exe
450	        alt = venv_dir / "Scripts" / "python.exe"
451	        if alt.exists():
452	            venv_python = alt
453	        else:
454	            print(
455	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: cannot find venv python "
456	                f"interpreter under {venv_dir!s}",
457	                file=sys.stderr,
458	            )
459	            return 1
460	
461	    for child in installable_children:
462	        result = subprocess.run(
463	            [str(venv_python), "-m", "pip", "install", "-e", str(child)],
464	            capture_output=True,
465	            text=True,
466	        )
467	        if result.returncode != 0:
468	            print(
469	                f"shop-templates bootstrap: `pip install -e {child!s}` "
470	                f"failed (exit {result.returncode}); stderr:\n"
471	                f"{result.stderr}",
472	                file=sys.stderr,
473	            )
474	            return result.returncode
475	
476	    return 0
477	
478	
479	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
480	# `update` subcommand
481	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
482	
483	
484	def _cmd_update(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
485	    # Update requires shop_type as an explicit argument so the
486	    # reconciliation step (scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031) can compare the
487	    # current contents of .claude/agents/ against the current canonical
488	    # role set for the right shop type. Inferring the shop type from
489	    # .claude/agents/ contents is unsafe: a hand-mutated shop may have
490	    # zero files whose names are in either canonical set.
491	    err = _validate_shop_type(args.shop_type, "update")
492	    if err is not None:
493	        return 2
494	
495	    # Validate target early. Scenario 8fe363bd46cb766c pins exit 2 +
496	    # stderr names "--target" + no traceback + every file under the
497	    # previously-bootstrapped shop's .claude/agents/ is byte+mtime
498	    # identical when --target is omitted. Without this guard,
499	    # args.target=None propagates into Path() and surfaces as a
500	    # TypeError from pathlib.
501	    err = _validate_target(args.target, "update")
502	    if err is not None:
503	        return 2
504	
505	    target = Path(args.target)
506	    shop_type: str = args.shop_type
507	    agents_dir = target / ".claude" / "agents"
508	    agents_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
509	
510	    canonical = set(_CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS[shop_type])
511	
512	    # The "naming convention" for bootstrap-managed agent files is:
513	    # filename = <role-name>.md where <role-name> is a name in the
514	    # canonical role set of SOME shop type (current or historical).
515	    # For reconciliation we treat any .md file in .claude/agents/ as a
516	    # candidate managed file; if its name is in the current canonical
517	    # role set for this shop type it stays (and gets re-poured); if
518	    # its name is in NEITHER canonical role set we leave it alone
519	    # (shop-authored content we don't recognize); if it matches a
520	    # canonical name from any role set (including the other shop
521	    # type's set, or a name previously in this set but no longer) we
522	    # remove it. This satisfies scenario 03b4e3fa31d72031's "remove
523	    # managed files whose canonical template no longer exists" while
524	    # leaving shop-authored files alone.
525	    all_canonical_names = set()
526	    for rs in _CANONICAL_ROLE_SETS.values():
527	        all_canonical_names.update(rs)
528	    # A file is "managed" if its base name (sans .md) is in the
529	    # historical set OR is a current canonical name we're about to
530	    # pour. For this implementation, treat managed = any .md file
531	    # whose name follows the role-prompt-template naming convention,
532	    # which we recognize via the heuristic: name contains "-" and is
533	    # all lowercase ASCII. That keeps shop-authored files like
534	    # "TODO.md" or "NOTES.md" out.
535	    def _is_managed_role_name(stem: str) -> bool:
536	        # Recognize names of the form "<prefix>-<suffix>" where both
537	        # halves are ASCII lowercase letters. This matches every
538	        # canonical role-name pattern shipped today (bc-implementer,
539	        # bc-reviewer, lead-po, lead-architect) and excludes typical
540	        # shop-authored markdown filenames.
541	        if "-" not in stem:
542	            return False
543	        if stem != stem.lower():
544	            return False
545	        if not all(c.islower() or c == "-" for c in stem):
546	            return False
547	        return True
548	
549	    # Step 1: remove managed files whose names are NOT in the current
550	    # canonical role set for this shop type.
551	    for child in list(agents_dir.iterdir()):
552	        if not child.is_file():
553	            continue
554	        if not child.name.endswith(".md"):
555	            continue
556	        stem = child.name[:-len(".md")]
557	        if stem in canonical:
558	            continue
559	        if _is_managed_role_name(stem):
560	            child.unlink()
561	
562	    # Step 2: re-pour every canonical role file. If a file is already
563	    # byte-equal to the canonical content, leave its on-disk
564	    # mtime alone (scenario 264322ae65312bc7).
565	    for role_name in canonical:
566	        body = _read_template(role_name)
567	        if body is None:
568	            print(
569	                f"shop-templates update: internal error — canonical "
570	                f"role template {role_name!r} not found in package data",
571	                file=sys.stderr,
572	            )
573	            return 1
574	        target_file = agents_dir / f"{role_name}.md"
575	        if target_file.exists():
576	            current = target_file.read_text()
577	            if current == body:
578	                continue
579	        target_file.write_text(body)
580	
581	    # Step 3: re-pour the canonical .claude/settings.json. Per scenarios
582	    # d29cd723439faae1 and d3066d4476d0a975: settings.json is treated as
583	    # bootstrap-managed (not init-only) — stale content is replaced and
584	    # already-current content is left byte-for-byte unchanged. This is
585	    # additive to the prior update contract: the non-touch invariant on
586	    # CLAUDE.md / .gitignore / .beads/ (scenarios 56a0ac7107ba5c15,
587	    # ca0f0a249d025267, 3f4d7d2256a97ae7, efae77e534588357) still holds.
588	    canonical_settings = read_claude_settings_template(shop_type)
589	    settings_file = target / ".claude" / "settings.json"
590	    settings_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
591	    if settings_file.exists() and settings_file.read_text() == canonical_settings:
592	        pass  # leave byte+mtime unchanged
593	    else:
594	        settings_file.write_text(canonical_settings)
595	
596	    return 0
597	
598	
599	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
600	# Argparse wiring
601	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
602	
603	
604	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
605	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="shop-templates")
606	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
607	
608	    list_cmd = sub.add_parser("list", help="list available template names")
609	    list_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_list)
610	
611	    show_cmd = sub.add_parser("show", help="print a named template to stdout")
612	    show_cmd.add_argument("name", help="template name (e.g. bc-implementer)")
613	    show_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_show)
614	
615	    bootstrap_cmd = sub.add_parser(
616	        "bootstrap",
617	        help="scaffold an existing repo with the canonical shop structure",
618	    )
619	    # `--shop-type` intentionally has no default. Scenario d6e935bca62d0039
620	    # requires that omitting it produces a non-zero exit and a usage
621	    # diagnostic naming the missing argument AND the accepted values.
622	    # We declare it as a regular optional argument (not required=True at
623	    # the argparse layer) so argparse does NOT short-circuit with its
624	    # own auto-generated "the following arguments are required:..."
625	    # message — we want our own diagnostic shape on stderr.
626	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
627	        "--shop-type",
628	        choices=None,  # validate manually so error shape is ours, not argparse's
629	        default=None,
630	        help='one of "bc" or "lead"',
631	    )
632	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
633	        "--shop-name",
634	        default=None,
635	        help="the shop's own name, e.g. shopsystem-messaging",
636	    )
637	    bootstrap_cmd.add_argument(
638	        "--target",
639	        default=None,
640	        help="path to the existing repository to scaffold",
641	    )
642	    bootstrap_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_bootstrap)
643	
644	    update_cmd = sub.add_parser(
645	        "update",
646	        help="re-pour bootstrap-managed agent files from current package data",
647	    )
648	    update_cmd.add_argument(
649	        "--target",
650	        default=None,
651	        help="path to the previously-bootstrapped repository",
652	    )
653	    update_cmd.add_argument(
654	        "--shop-type",
655	        default=None,
656	        help='one of "bc" or "lead" (must match the original bootstrap)',
657	    )
658	    update_cmd.set_defaults(func=_cmd_update)
659	
660	    return parser
661	
662	
663	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
664	    parser = build_parser()
665	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
666	    return args.func(args)
667	
668	
669	if __name__ == "__main__":
670	    sys.exit(main())
671
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-19 14:25:50Z)**_

```text
1	# {{SHOP_NAME}} — BC shop instructions
2	
3	This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** Bounded Context shop. As an agent
4	operating in this repo, you are operating inside a **BC shop** that uses
5	the inbox/outbox message protocol from §4 of the shop-system spec.
6	
7	## Who you are — router for bc-implementer and bc-reviewer subagents
8	
9	By default you are the **router** for this BC shop. The two role-discipline
10	positions — **Implementer** and **Reviewer** per the shop-system spec §4 /
11	§4.4 — are dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request
12	and delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.
13	
14	The canonical role set for this shop type is:
15	
16	- **bc-implementer** — reads inbox messages, applies the sufficiency check
17	  matching the message type, and either emits `clarify` via `shop-msg
18	  respond clarify` or does the work (feature file under `features/`, step
19	  defs in `tests/conftest.py`, implementation under `src/`, BDD passing).
20	
21	- **bc-reviewer** — dispatched AFTER the implementer's turn on an
22	  `assign_scenarios` (or scenario-carrying `request_bugfix`) message has
23	  finished and the BC is in its post-work state with no outbox file yet.
24	  The reviewer is the sole role authorized to emit `work_done` for
25	  scenario-based work.
26	
27	Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/bc-implementer.md` and
28	`.claude/agents/bc-reviewer.md`. These are inline copies of the canonical
29	templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not edit them
30	independently of the canonical source.
31	
32	## BC inbox / outbox protocol
33	
34	- **Inbox** (`inbox/`) holds messages from the lead shop. Filename
35	  convention: `<work_id>.yaml`. One file per dispatch.
36	- **Outbox** (`outbox/`) holds this BC's responses. Filename convention:
37	  `<work_id>-<response_type>.yaml`. The `shop-msg respond` CLI builds
38	  and validates these — never write outbox YAML by hand.
39	- A message is considered **unprocessed** when there is no outbox file
40	  for its `work_id`. Use `shop-msg pending inbox` to identify
41	  unprocessed work, and `shop-msg read inbox` to read a specific
42	  inbox message.
43	
44	## Do not
45	
46	- **No editing the BC's inbox/outbox by hand.** `shop-msg send` writes
47	  inboxes (lead shop's job); `shop-msg respond` writes outboxes
48	  (BC's job). Both validate against the schema.
49	- **No skipping the sufficiency check.** Each BC role template carries
50	  a sufficiency check matching the inbound message type; honor it.
51	
52	## Beads (bd) discipline
53	
54	This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. The
55	inbound `work_id` on each inbox message is the lead shop's bead ID;
56	this BC's own follow-up findings (mechanism observations, escaped
57	risks, deferred work) are filed as beads in this repo.
58	
59	- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
60	  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
61	- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
62	  an issue, and `bd close <id>` to mark work complete. Do NOT track
63	  work in markdown TODO lists or alternative trackers.
64	- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
65	  session.
66	
67	## Session start: arming the inbox watcher via the in-session Monitor
68	
69	This shop is reactive on session start: when a new message lands in
70	`inbox/`, the router must learn about it without polling. The activation
71	mechanism is the in-session **Monitor** tool — not a `SessionStart` hook
72	in `.claude/settings.json`. (Earlier iterations of this template tried
73	the hook path; Claude Code awaits `SessionStart` hooks synchronously, so
74	a foreground `inotifywait -m` pipeline never returns and session startup
75	hangs. The Monitor tool is the documented in-session primitive that
76	delivers the streaming-stdout-as-notifications semantic the hook was
77	faking, without blocking startup.)
78	
79	**At session start, the router must arm the in-session Monitor tool on
80	the following pipeline (watch target: `inbox/`):**
81	
82	```
83	stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to inbox/
84	```
85	
86	Before arming the Monitor, the router must verify that both
87	`inotifywait` and `stdbuf` are on PATH (for example, via
88	`command -v inotifywait` and `command -v stdbuf`). If either executable
89	is missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a
90	visible diagnostic naming the missing prerequisite — do not silently
91	fall back to a no-watcher state, and do not arm the watcher via a
92	`SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` as a fallback. A
93	no-watcher session loses the shop's reactivity invariant; the operator
94	needs to see the diagnostic and install the missing package.
95	
96	### Host prerequisites
97	
98	The Monitor activation pipeline named above depends on two host-level
99	packages being present on PATH:
100	
101	- **inotify-tools** — provides the `inotifywait` binary used to watch
102	  the inbound mailbox surface for new messages.
103	- **coreutils** — provides `stdbuf`, used to set line-buffered output
104	  on the `inotifywait` invocation so events stream as they happen
105	  rather than batching into a pipe buffer.
106	
107	Install both packages through your distro's package manager (e.g.
108	`apt-get install inotify-tools coreutils` on Debian/Ubuntu).
109
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# {{SHOP_NAME}} — lead shop instructions
2	
3	This repository is the **{{SHOP_NAME}}** lead shop. As an agent operating
4	in this repo, you are operating inside a **lead shop** under the shop-system
5	spec.
6	
7	## Who you are — router for lead-po and lead-architect subagents
8	
9	By default you are the **router** for this lead shop. The two judgment
10	roles — **PO** and **Architect** per §3 of the shop-system spec — are
11	dispatched as subagents. Your job is to classify each request and
12	delegate; do not enact the roles yourself.
13	
14	The canonical role set for this shop type is:
15	
16	- **lead-po** — authors or sharpens Gherkin scenarios; drafts briefs or
17	  PDRs; responds to BC `clarify` on scope or vocabulary.
18	
19	- **lead-architect** — selects a message-type vehicle; composes
20	  `shop-msg send`; verifies BC pre-state empirically; responds to BC
21	  `clarify` on architecture; reconciles scenario registers; drafts ADRs;
22	  makes BC decomposition decisions.
23	
24	Subagent definitions live at `.claude/agents/lead-po.md` and
25	`.claude/agents/lead-architect.md`. These are inline copies of the
26	canonical templates shipped by the `shopsystem-templates` BC; do not
27	edit them independently of the canonical source.
28	
29	## How feature requests get handled
30	
31	When a user request implies a new BC capability, a tightening, or a flat
32	change to an existing BC:
33	
34	1. **PO authors intent first.** Brief → PDR → Gherkin scenarios.
35	   Implementation discussion before authored scenarios is the failure
36	   mode §3 exists to prevent.
37	2. **Architect verifies the relevant BC's pre-state empirically.**
38	   Reading the BC's code is hypothesis; running it is fact. Construct a
39	   concrete input that exhibits (or fails to exhibit) the behavior;
40	   observe; cite the demonstration in the dispatch description.
41	3. **Architect applies the message-type discriminator:**
42	   - No capability → `assign_scenarios`.
43	   - Capability exists but unpinned → `request_bugfix`.
44	   - Flat (refactor / doc / value-only) → `request_maintenance`.
45	4. **Architect dispatches via `shop-msg send`.** Never write inbox/outbox
46	   YAML by hand.
47	
48	## Do not
49	
50	- **No implementation code in this repo.** This is the lead shop; code
51	  lives in BCs.
52	- **No skipping the discriminator.** If a request smells like open-ended
53	  "what should we build?" — route it back through the process. First
54	  question: *what scenarios pin this?*
55	
56	## Beads (bd) discipline
57	
58	This shop uses **bd (beads)** for its work-tracking registry. Lead-shop
59	bead IDs are the canonical `work_id` values that flow outward into
60	`shop-msg send` when dispatching to a BC.
61	
62	- Run `bd prime` at the start of a working session to load the full
63	  workflow context and command reference for this repo.
64	- Use `bd ready` to find available work, `bd show <id>` to inspect
65	  an issue, `bd update <id> --claim` to claim work, and `bd close <id>`
66	  to mark work complete. Do NOT track work in markdown TODO lists or
67	  alternative trackers.
68	- Use `bd remember` for persistent knowledge that should outlive the
69	  session.
70	
71	## Session start: arming the BC-outbox watcher via the in-session Monitor
72	
73	This shop is reactive on session start: when a BC drops a response
74	into one of its outbox directories, the router must learn about it
75	without polling. The activation mechanism is the in-session **Monitor**
76	tool — not a `SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json`. (Earlier
77	iterations of this template tried the hook path; Claude Code awaits
78	`SessionStart` hooks synchronously, so a foreground `inotifywait -m`
79	pipeline never returns and session startup hangs. The Monitor tool is
80	the documented in-session primitive that delivers the
81	streaming-stdout-as-notifications semantic the hook was faking, without
82	blocking startup.)
83	
84	**At session start, the router must arm the in-session Monitor tool on
85	the following pipeline (watch target: `repos/*/outbox/`):**
86	
87	```
88	stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to repos/*/outbox/
89	```
90	
91	Before arming the Monitor, the router must verify that both
92	`inotifywait` and `stdbuf` are on PATH (for example, via
93	`command -v inotifywait` and `command -v stdbuf`). If either executable
94	is missing, the router must refuse to arm the Monitor and surface a
95	visible diagnostic naming the missing prerequisite — do not silently
96	fall back to a no-watcher state, and do not arm the watcher via a
97	`SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` as a fallback. A
98	no-watcher session loses the shop's reactivity invariant; the operator
99	needs to see the diagnostic and install the missing package.
100	
101	The lead shop's canonical `.claude/settings.json` still declares a
102	`SessionStart` hook for `bd prime` (short-lived, returns cleanly — a
103	legitimate synchronous hook usage); the activation hook itself has
104	moved to the router's Monitor invocation described here.
105	
106	### Host prerequisites
107	
108	The Monitor activation pipeline named above depends on two host-level
109	packages being present on PATH:
110	
111	- **inotify-tools** — provides the `inotifywait` binary used to watch
112	  the per-BC outbox directories for new responses.
113	- **coreutils** — provides `stdbuf`, used to set line-buffered output
114	  on the `inotifywait` invocation so events stream as they happen
115	  rather than batching into a pipe buffer.
116	
117	Install both packages through your distro's package manager (e.g.
118	`apt-get install inotify-tools coreutils` on Debian/Ubuntu).
119
```

```text
1	"""Shared fixtures and pytest-bdd step definitions for shopsystem-templates.
2	
3	The scenarios under features/ pin the BC's outward contract — the
4	`shop-templates` CLI surface and the structural section headers each
5	role-template markdown file carries. The CLI is invoked via subprocess
6	(the same boundary downstream callers use); content checks read the
7	template via the same CLI subcommand so any drift between the CLI's
8	view of the package data and the file system surfaces here.
9	
10	Style mirrors shopsystem-messaging/tests/conftest.py and
11	shopsystem-scenarios/tests/conftest.py — subprocess + a `context` dict
12	fixture carrying cross-step state.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import re
17	import subprocess
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	import pytest
22	from pytest_bdd import given, parsers, then, when
23	
24	
25	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# Shared cross-step state
27	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
28	
29	
30	@pytest.fixture
31	def context() -> dict:
32	    return {}
33	
34	
35	def _run_shop_templates(*args: str) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
36	    return subprocess.run(
37	        ["shop-templates", *args],
38	        capture_output=True,
39	        text=True,
40	    )
41	
42	
43	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Given steps
45	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
46	
47	
48	@given(
49	    parsers.parse(
50	        'a template name "{name}" that is in the list output by '
51	        '"shop-templates list"'
52	    )
53	)
54	def given_template_name_in_list(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
55	    # Verify empirically that the name appears in `shop-templates list`
56	    # output — the Given's contract is part of the scenario's premise and
57	    # a future drop of a template would silently undermine the When/Then
58	    # steps if we didn't check it here.
59	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
60	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
61	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
62	    )
63	    listed = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
64	    assert name in listed, (
65	        f"premise of Given violated: {name!r} is not in shop-templates list "
66	        f"output {listed!r}"
67	    )
68	    context["available_templates"] = listed
69	
70	
71	@given(
72	    parsers.parse(
73	        'a template name "{name}" that is not in the list output by '
74	        '"shop-templates list"'
75	    )
76	)
77	def given_template_name_not_in_list(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
78	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
79	    assert result.returncode == 0, (
80	        f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
81	    )
82	    listed = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
83	    assert name not in listed, (
84	        f"premise of Given violated: {name!r} unexpectedly appears in "
85	        f"shop-templates list output {listed!r}"
86	    )
87	    context["available_templates"] = listed
88	
89	
90	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
91	# When steps
92	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
93	
94	
95	@when(parsers.parse('I run "shop-templates list"'))
96	def when_run_list(context: dict) -> None:
97	    result = _run_shop_templates("list")
98	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
99	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
100	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
```
</details></tool-use>

100	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
101	
102	
103	@when(parsers.parse('I run "shop-templates show {name}"'))
104	def when_run_show(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
105	    result = _run_shop_templates("show", name)
106	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
107	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
108	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
109	    context["shown_template_name"] = name
110	
111	
112	@when(
113	    parsers.parse(
114	        'I read the {name} template via "shop-templates show {name_again}"'
115	    )
116	)
117	def when_read_template_via_show(name: str, name_again: str, context: dict) -> None:
118	    # Belt-and-braces: the two name slots must agree, otherwise the
119	    # scenario text is internally inconsistent and any Then assertion
120	    # would be assertion-by-coincidence.
121	    assert name == name_again, (
122	        f"scenario inconsistency: 'the {name} template' but "
123	        f"'shop-templates show {name_again}'"
124	    )
125	    result = _run_shop_templates("show", name)
126	    context["cli_returncode"] = result.returncode
127	    context["cli_stdout"] = result.stdout
128	    context["cli_stderr"] = result.stderr
129	    context["shown_template_name"] = name
130	    # The Then-steps for these scenarios refer to "the content" — keep an
131	    # explicit alias so the assertion language matches the feature text.
132	    context["template_content"] = result.stdout
133	
134	
135	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
136	# Then steps — generic CLI assertions
137	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
138	
139	
140	@then("the exit code is 0")
141	def then_exit_code_is_zero(context: dict) -> None:
142	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]
143	    assert rc == 0, (
144	        f"expected exit code 0; got {rc}; stderr:\n{context.get('cli_stderr', '')}"
145	    )
146	
147	
148	@then("the exit code is non-zero")
149	def then_exit_code_is_nonzero(context: dict) -> None:
150	    rc = context["cli_returncode"]
151	    assert rc != 0, (
152	        f"expected non-zero exit; got {rc}; stderr:\n{context.get('cli_stderr', '')}"
153	    )
154	
155	
156	@then("stderr is empty")
157	def then_stderr_is_empty(context: dict) -> None:
158	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
159	    assert stderr == "", f"expected empty stderr; got {stderr!r}"
160	
161	
162	@then("stdout is empty")
163	def then_stdout_is_empty(context: dict) -> None:
164	    stdout = context["cli_stdout"]
165	    assert stdout == "", f"expected empty stdout; got {stdout!r}"
166	
167	
168	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
169	# Then steps — shop-templates list shape
170	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
171	
172	
173	@then(
174	    parsers.re(
175	        r'stdout is exactly the four names '
176	        r'"(?P<n1>[^"]+)", "(?P<n2>[^"]+)", "(?P<n3>[^"]+)", "(?P<n4>[^"]+)", '
177	        r'one per line in that sorted order'
178	    )
179	)
180	def then_stdout_is_exactly_four_names_sorted(
181	    n1: str, n2: str, n3: str, n4: str, context: dict
182	) -> None:
183	    expected = [n1, n2, n3, n4]
184	    # Pin the sorted-order contract explicitly: if the scenario text
185	    # claims "sorted order" but the literal sequence supplied is not
186	    # actually sorted, the scenario is internally inconsistent and the
187	    # downstream assertion would be meaningless.
188	    assert expected == sorted(expected), (
189	        f"scenario premise violated: supplied names {expected!r} are not "
190	        f"in sorted order"
191	    )
192	    stdout = context["cli_stdout"]
193	    # The CLI uses print() per line, which emits a trailing newline after
194	    # the last line. Split on newlines and drop the final empty entry
195	    # that comes from that trailing newline.
196	    lines = stdout.split("\n")
197	    if lines and lines[-1] == "":
198	        lines = lines[:-1]
199	    assert lines == expected, (
200	        f"expected stdout lines {expected!r}; got {lines!r}"
201	    )
202	
203	
204	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
205	# Then steps — shop-templates show byte-for-byte equivalence
206	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
207	
208	
209	@then(
210	    "stdout equals the package-data file contents for that template, "
211	    "byte-for-byte (no extra trailing newline beyond what the file itself carries)"
212	)
213	def then_stdout_equals_package_data_byte_for_byte(context: dict) -> None:
214	    # Re-read the underlying template through the same package-data
215	    # boundary the CLI uses. Importing inside the step (rather than at
216	    # module top) keeps the conftest cleanly isolated from src/ — only
217	    # this assertion needs the boundary helper, and only at run time.
218	    from shop_templates.cli import _read_template
219	
220	    name = context["shown_template_name"]
221	    expected = _read_template(name)
222	    assert expected is not None, (
223	        f"premise of Then violated: package data has no template named {name!r}"
224	    )
225	    actual = context["cli_stdout"]
226	    assert actual == expected, (
227	        f"shop-templates show {name} did not emit byte-identical content "
228	        f"to the package-data file.\n"
229	        f"len(expected)={len(expected)} len(actual)={len(actual)}\n"
230	        f"expected_repr_tail={expected[-40:]!r}\n"
231	        f"actual_repr_tail={actual[-40:]!r}"
232	    )
233	
234	
235	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
236	# Then steps — shop-templates show unknown-template error shape
237	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
238	
239	
240	@then(parsers.parse('stderr names the offending input "{name}"'))
241	def then_stderr_names_offending_input(name: str, context: dict) -> None:
242	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
243	    assert name in stderr, (
244	        f"expected stderr to name offending input {name!r}; got:\n{stderr}"
245	    )
246	
247	
248	@then(
249	    "stderr cites the list of available templates so the caller can recover"
250	)
251	def then_stderr_cites_available_templates(context: dict) -> None:
252	    stderr = context["cli_stderr"]
253	    # "cites the list" is satisfied when at least one real template name
254	    # appears in stderr — the caller can derive the available set from
255	    # there. We require every known template name to appear, which is
256	    # the strongest reading of "cites the list" and matches the
257	    # implementation's `Available: <comma-joined names>` shape.
258	    available = context.get("available_templates")
259	    if available is None:
260	        # Recover the list directly if a preceding Given did not stash it.
261	        result = _run_shop_templates("list")
262	        assert result.returncode == 0, (
263	            f"shop-templates list failed; stderr:\n{result.stderr}"
264	        )
265	        available = result.stdout.strip().splitlines()
266	    for name in available:
267	        assert name in stderr, (
268	            f"expected stderr to cite available template {name!r}; got:\n{stderr}"
269	        )
270	
271	
272	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
273	# Then steps — template content / section-header assertions
274	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
275	
276	
277	@then(
278	    parsers.parse(
279	        'the content contains a "{section_header}" section header'
280	    )
281	)
282	def then_content_contains_section_header(
283	    section_header: str, context: dict
284	) -> None:
285	    content = context["template_content"]
286	    assert section_header in content, (
287	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing "
288	        f"section header {section_header!r}"
289	    )
290	
291	
292	@then(
293	    'the content names "empirical" pre-state verification as the discipline '
294	    "for choosing between assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, and request_maintenance"
295	)
296	def then_content_names_empirical_pre_state_verification(context: dict) -> None:
297	    content = context["template_content"].lower()
298	    # Pin Finding 17 / S16: the discriminator is "empirical". The
299	    # surrounding message-type vocabulary must also appear so this isn't
```

300	    # satisfied by an unrelated use of "empirical" elsewhere in the

314	        )
315	
316	
317	@then(
318	    'the content distinguishes "reading code" (hypothesis) from "running it" '
319	    "(fact) as the basis for that choice"
320	)
321	def then_content_distinguishes_reading_from_running(context: dict) -> None:
322	    content = context["template_content"].lower()
323	    # The Finding 17 prose introduced the
324	    # "reading code is hypothesis / running it is fact" distinction. Pin
325	    # both halves of the contrast — either alone is satisfiable by an
326	    # unrelated sentence, but both together unambiguously identify this
327	    # discipline.
328	    assert "reading code" in content, (
329	        "template must reference 'reading code' as the hypothesis side of "
330	        "the pre-state-verification distinction (Finding 17 / S16)"
331	    )
332	    assert "running it" in content, (
333	        "template must reference 'running it' as the fact side of the "
334	        "pre-state-verification distinction (Finding 17 / S16)"
335	    )
336	    assert "hypothesis" in content, (
337	        "template must label the 'reading code' side as a hypothesis "
338	        "(Finding 17 / S16)"
339	    )
340	    assert "fact" in content, (
341	        "template must label the 'running it' side as a fact "
342	        "(Finding 17 / S16)"
343	    )
344	
345	
346	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
347	# Then steps — BC @scenario_hash pre-state enumeration discipline
348	# (lead-dbc scenarios 0d384c6b92004c8d, 48dd1f01012efafe, 22cbdf7cc9e917ca,
349	# 744cd4a4532c28d7)
350	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
351	#
352	# These steps pin that the lead-architect template names @scenario_hash
353	# enumeration (via grep over features/*.feature) as a discrete, required
354	# pre-state step whenever a dispatch retires, supersedes, or contradicts
355	# prior BC-side coverage — and that the discipline applies on every
356	# dispatch in a clarify-correction chain, not only the initial one.
357	
358	
359	@then(
360	    'the content names "@scenario_hash" as a pre-state surface the architect '
361	    "must verify before composing a dispatch that retires, supersedes, or "
362	    "contradicts prior BC-side coverage"
363	)
364	def then_content_names_scenario_hash_pre_state_surface(context: dict) -> None:

603	def then_content_directs_hash_reference_or_retirement(context: dict) -> None:
604	    content = context["template_content"]

609	    # template has "explicit" in unrelated contexts, so we require "retire" or
610	    # "retirement" as a stronger signal.

678	#
679	# Scenarios lead-kq0 (f1ac9534c1d58318, e522f7393dfcd1c1, a481db51463526d2,
680	# a6b3e510821aba52) pin the structural ordering of the lead-po and
681	# lead-architect templates: role identity (depth-1 #) and default-posture
682	# header (depth-2 ##) must lead the document, and procedural shop-msg CLI
683	# content must come after both the posture header AND after every §3.2
684	# activity has been named at least once. The §3.2 activity lists for each
685	# role are hard-coded below because the scenarios reference them by index
686	# ("scenario 10" / "scenario 14") within the dispatched feature file.
687	
688	_LEAD_PO_ACTIVITY_NAMES_FROM_SCENARIO_10 = (
689	    "Interview stakeholder",
690	    "Maintain product brief",
691	    "Write PDR for new functionality",
692	    "Write Gherkin scenarios",
693	    "Respond to BC `clarify`",
694	)
695

697	    "Write ADRs",
698	    "Maintain structurizr workspace",
699	    "Collaborate with PO on BC decomposition",
700	    "Assign scenarios to BCs",
701	    "Reconcile scenario registers",

703	    # either "Send `request_bugfix`" appears OR both message-type names

705	    # purposes — that is the substring all renderings share.

707	    "Read a BC-shop's card via `request_shop_card`",
708	    "Respond to BC `clarify`",

712	def _first_index(content: str, needle: str) -> int:
713	    idx = content.find(needle)
714	    assert idx >= 0, f"expected substring {needle!r} to appear in content"
715	    return idx
716

718	@then(parsers.parse('a "{header}" identity header appears in the output'))
719	def then_identity_header_appears(header: str, context: dict) -> None:
720	    # The identity header is the depth-1 (#) heading that opens the role
721	    # prompt — the bare-presence check; ordering is asserted by separate
722	    # byte-offset steps below.
723	    content = context["template_content"]
724	    assert header in content, (
725	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} is missing identity "
726	        f"header {header!r}"
727	    )
728	    # Stash the offset for the byte-offset Then steps that follow.
729	    context["identity_header_offset"] = content.index(header)

780	#
781	# Scenarios dddd6c3b2eed7e45 (PO) and 0aea22a97e63d4f8 (Architect) pin
782	# that each §3.2 activity must be NAMED in the template. The qualifier
783	# variant additionally asserts that a qualifier word appears in proximity
784	# to the activity name. The alternation variant accepts either the
785	# "Send `request_bugfix`" phrasing or both bare message-type names.
786	
787	
788	@then(
789	    parsers.re(

791	    )

793	def then_content_names_activity(activity: str, context: dict) -> None:
794	    content = context["template_content"]
795	    assert activity in content, (
796	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
797	        f"activity {activity!r}"
798	    )
799	
800	
801	@then(
802	    parsers.re(
803	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)" with the '
804	        r'qualifier "(?P<qualifier>[^"]+)"$'
805	    )
806	)
807	def then_content_names_activity_with_qualifier(
808	    activity: str, qualifier: str, context: dict
809	) -> None:
810	    content = context["template_content"]
811	    assert activity in content, (
812	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
813	        f"activity {activity!r}"
814	    )
815	    # The qualifier must appear in the same paragraph / contiguous block as
816	    # the activity name. We approximate "same block" as: somewhere within
817	    # 200 bytes after the activity name OR on the same line. That's broad
818	    # enough to allow a parenthetical, a sentence-following clause, or the
819	    # next sentence, but tight enough that the qualifier is contextually
820	    # bound to the activity rather than an unrelated mention elsewhere.
821	    idx = content.index(activity)
822	    window = content[idx : idx + 200 + len(activity)]
823	    assert qualifier in window, (
824	        f"qualifier {qualifier!r} must appear near activity {activity!r} in "
825	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r}; window after "
826	        f"activity:\n{window!r}"
827	    )
828	
829	
830	@then(
831	    parsers.re(
832	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)" with the '
833	        r'qualifier "(?P<q1>[^"]+)" or "(?P<q2>[^"]+)"$'
834	    )
835	)
836	def then_content_names_activity_with_either_qualifier(
837	    activity: str, q1: str, q2: str, context: dict
838	) -> None:
839	    content = context["template_content"]
840	    assert activity in content, (
841	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} does not name §3.2 "
842	        f"activity {activity!r}"
843	    )
844	    idx = content.index(activity)
845	    window = content[idx : idx + 200 + len(activity)]
846	    assert q1 in window or q2 in window, (
847	        f"at least one of qualifiers {q1!r} / {q2!r} must appear near "
848	        f"activity {activity!r} in template "
849	        f"{context.get('shown_template_name')!r}; window after activity:\n"
850	        f"{window!r}"
851	    )
852	
853	
854	@then(
855	    parsers.re(
856	        r'^the content names the activity "(?P<activity>[^"]+)" or '
857	        r'equivalently mentions both "(?P<token_a>[^"]+)" and '
858	        r'"(?P<token_b>[^"]+)" as dispatch vehicles$'
859	    )
860	)
861	def then_content_names_activity_or_equivalent_pair(
862	    activity: str, token_a: str, token_b: str, context: dict
863	) -> None:
864	    content = context["template_content"]
865	    # Either: the explicit "Send `request_bugfix`" phrasing appears,
866	    # OR both message-type names appear somewhere in the content. The
867	    # latter satisfies the §3.2 activity entry "Send `request_bugfix` /
868	    # `request_maintenance`" without forcing the template to repeat the
869	    # "Send " prefix.
870	    if activity in content:
871	        return
872	    assert token_a in content and token_b in content, (
873	        f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r} must either name "
874	        f"activity {activity!r} OR mention both {token_a!r} and {token_b!r} "
875	        f"as dispatch vehicles; neither condition held"
876	    )
877	
878	
879	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
880	# Given + Then steps — §3.2 activity guidance coverage (scenarios
881	# 9a9421ad59ee5d67 and 5ccb3fb1122f9341)
882	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
883	#
884	# The Given step stashes the activity list (literal phrasing as it
885	# appears in the dispatched scenario) on context. The Then step iterates
886	# that list and, for each activity, verifies either:
887	#   (a) the activity name appears on a line that also contains a colon
888	#       followed by non-whitespace prose (one-line guidance), OR
889	#   (b) the activity name appears as a subsection heading whose body
890	#       carries at least one sentence of prose, OR
891	#   (c) within a 240-byte window after the activity name the literal
892	#       phrase "guidance pending" appears (case-insensitive).
893	# The "no bare list item" assertion checks that no line of the form
894	# "- <activity>" or "* <activity>" exists without trailing colon/prose.
895	
896	
897	def _activity_block_satisfies_guidance(content: str, activity: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
898	    """Return (ok, reason) for the activity-guidance check.
899
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
900	    The check accepts any of three shapes:
901	      1. "guidance pending" within a 240-byte window after the activity
902	         name (case-insensitive).
903	      2. A line that names the activity AND contains a colon followed by
904	         non-whitespace prose on the same line — one-line guidance.
905	      3. A subsection heading (## or ### or ####) whose text names the
906	         activity, with at least one non-blank, non-heading line after
907	         it in the same subsection — multi-line guidance.
908	    """
909	    lower_content = content.lower()
910	    lower_activity = activity.lower()
911	
912	    # Shape 1: explicit "guidance pending" marker near the activity.
913	    idx = lower_content.find(lower_activity)
914	    while idx != -1:
915	        window = lower_content[idx : idx + 240 + len(lower_activity)]
916	        if "guidance pending" in window:
917	            return True, "guidance-pending marker present"
918	        idx = lower_content.find(lower_activity, idx + 1)
919	
920	    # Shapes 2 and 3: walk line by line.
921	    lines = content.splitlines()
922	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
923	        if activity not in line:
924	            continue
925	        stripped = line.lstrip()
926	        # Shape 3: heading naming the activity.
927	        if stripped.startswith("#"):
928	            # Look for at least one non-blank, non-heading line after.
929	            for j in range(i + 1, len(lines)):
930	                follow = lines[j].strip()
931	                if follow.startswith("#"):
932	                    break  # next heading reached; no body in this subsection
933	                if follow:
934	                    return True, "subsection-with-body"
935	            # Heading with no body — fall through to bare-list check.
936	            continue
937	        # Shape 2: one-line guidance. Activity name followed by a colon
938	        # and then non-whitespace on the same line.
939	        after = line.split(activity, 1)[1]
940	        if ":" in after:
941	            tail = after.split(":", 1)[1]
942	            if tail.strip():
943	                return True, "one-line guidance"
944	        # Bullet line with just the activity name and nothing else after.
945	        # Continue scanning — another occurrence might satisfy.
946	    return False, "no satisfying block found"

1147	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1148	# Step definitions — inter-shop messaging encapsulation (lead-bwu)
1149	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1150	#
1151	# Scenarios 17-25 of features/inter_shop_messaging_encapsulation.feature
1152	# pin two invariants across the four canonical templates and the two
1153	# CLAUDE.md files of the shopsystem product:
1154	#
1155	#   1. CLI-only mailbox access: every prescribed inbox/outbox operation
1156	#      names a `shop-msg` subcommand. No filesystem-direct inspection,
1157	#      no filename-convention reasoning, no set-difference-on-filenames
1158	#      characterization of "unprocessed".
1159	#
1160	#   2. bd-decoupling: bd participation is the local work-tracker
1161	#      concern of each shop; no template or CLAUDE.md makes a bd
1162	#      command a precondition for a shop-msg invocation, and no
1163	#      template names `--bd-ref` (the flag has been replaced with the
1164	#      optional, tracker-neutral `--provenance-ref`).
1165	#
1166	# Scenarios 21, 22, 25 reach OUTSIDE the BC root to read the lead-shop
1167	# CLAUDE.md at <product>/CLAUDE.md and the BC's own CLAUDE.md at
1168	# <product>/repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md. The lead's dispatch
1169	# (lead-bwu) explicitly names both files as in-scope surfaces — this
1170	# read is sanctioned by the message.
1171	
1172	
1173	def _product_root() -> Path:
1174	    """Return the lead-shop (product) root directory.
1175	
1176	    The BC root for shopsystem-templates is two levels below the product
1177	    root: <product>/repos/shopsystem-templates. Resolve by walking up
1178	    from this file (tests/conftest.py inside the BC) two more levels.
1179	    """
1180	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent
1181	
1182	
1183	def _read_file_at_product_path(relative_path: str) -> str:
1184	    """Read a file path that is given relative to the product root.
1185	
1186	    The scenarios spell paths as "CLAUDE.md" (lead shop) and
1187	    "repos/shopsystem-templates/CLAUDE.md" (BC). Both anchor at the
1188	    product root.
1189	    """
1190	    target = _product_root() / relative_path
1191	    return target.read_text()
1192

1195	# Given steps — file-at-path premise (scenarios 21, 22, 25)
1196	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

1336	
1337	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1338	# Then steps — instructional negations (no "read the file directly"-style
1339	# guidance with respect to inbox/outbox)
1340	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
1341	#
1342	# These steps assert that the template/file does not instruct the
1343	# reader to perform a non-shop-msg filesystem operation on the
1344	# inbox/outbox. The check is conservative: forbid the literal
1345	# substrings that would betray such instruction (the same shapes the
1346	# scenario text enumerates), and additionally forbid common bypass
1347	# phrasings ("cat <BC root>/inbox", "open inbox/", etc.) when they
1348	# co-occur with the mailbox words.
1349	
1350	
1351	_INBOX_OUTBOX_TOKENS = ("inbox", "outbox")
1352	
1353	
1354	def _content_for_target(context: dict, target_label: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
1355	    """Return (content, identifier) for assertion error messages.
1356	
1357	    `target_label` is 'content' (template) or 'file' (CLAUDE.md).
1358	    """
1359	    if target_label == "content":
1360	        return (
1361	            context["template_content"],
1362	            f"template {context.get('shown_template_name')!r}",
1363	        )
1364	    return (
1365	        context["file_content"],
1366	        f"file {context.get('file_relative_path')!r}",
1367	    )
1368	
1369	
1370	def _assert_no_direct_inbox_outbox_instruction(
1371	    content: str, identifier: str
1372	) -> None:
1373	    """Common check for 'does not instruct the reader to ... inbox/outbox'.
1374	
1375	    The scenarios call out the same shapes from different angles. The
1376	    check below covers the union conservatively:
1377	
1378	    - Literal phrases like "read the file directly", "read the YAML
1379	      directly", "open the file" combined with inbox/outbox proximity.
1380	    - Direct cat/ls/grep/open verbs with inbox/outbox as direct object.
1381	
1382	    A line is an offense only if it gives an action on a mailbox path
1383	    without naming a shop-msg subcommand on the same line. That keeps
1384	    sentences like "shop-msg read inbox reads the file" from
1385	    incidentally triggering: the shop-msg subcommand name is present.
1386	    """
1387	    offenders: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
1388	    for lineno, line in enumerate(content.splitlines(), start=1):
1389	        lower = line.lower()
1390	        # Quick reject: no inbox/outbox mention → no offense possible.
1391	        if not any(tok in lower for tok in _INBOX_OUTBOX_TOKENS):
1392	            continue
1393	        # If the line already names a shop-msg subcommand, the inbox/
1394	        # outbox reference is within a sanctioned context. Skip.
1395	        if "shop-msg" in lower:
1396	            continue
1397	        # Offending phrasings: direct filesystem verbs / "read directly"
1398	        # variants targeting the mailbox.
1399	        triggers = (
1400	            "read the file directly",
1401	            "read the yaml directly",
1402	            "read the file",
1403	            "read the yaml",
1404	            "open the file",
1405	            "open the yaml",
1406	            "cat inbox",
1407	            "cat outbox",
1408	            "cat <bc root>/inbox",
1409	            "cat <bc root>/outbox",
1410	            "ls inbox",
1411	            "ls outbox",
1412	            "grep inbox",
1413	            "grep outbox",
1414	            "inspect the file",
1415	            "inspect the yaml",
1416	        )
1417	        if any(t in lower for t in triggers):
1418	            offenders.append((lineno, line.rstrip()))
1419	            continue
1420	        # Path-shape offenses: a sentence that names an inbox/outbox
1421	        # path token (`<BC root>/inbox/`, `inbox/`, `outbox/`) as the
1422	        # target of an inspection verb without a shop-msg cite. Look
1423	        # for action verbs in the same line.
1424	        action_verbs = (
1425	            "open ", "cat ", "ls ", "grep ", "inspect ", "view ",
1426	            "edit ", "tail ", "head ",
1427	        )
1428	        path_tokens = (
1429	            "<bc root>/inbox", "<bc root>/outbox",
1430	            "repos/<bc>/inbox", "repos/<bc>/outbox",
1431	        )
1432	        if any(v in lower for v in action_verbs) and any(
1433	            p in lower for p in path_tokens
1434	        ):
1435	            offenders.append((lineno, line.rstrip()))
1436	    assert not offenders, (
1437	        f"{identifier} contains instruction(s) to operate on inbox/outbox "
1438	        f"directly (without shop-msg) — forbidden:\n"
1439	        + "\n".join(f"  line {n}: {l!r}" for n, l in offenders)
1440	    )

1446	)
